tweets.text:
The Middle East risk is no longer just an oil story.

It is increasingly a hidden-inputs and industrial supply-chain story.

Reuters, citing Barclays research, reported that after mapping 2.8 million trade corridors across more than 1,200 products, Barclays identified 30 critical goods for which the Middle East accounts for more than 10% of global trade value.  

That matters because the exposure runs well beyond crude and LNG.

Barclays highlighted major Middle East shares in products such as limestone flux, sulphur, nitrogen fertilizers, and key petrochemical inputs, showing that the region is deeply embedded in chemicals, construction, and agriculture, not just energy. Reuters-linked summaries of the note say the Middle East supplies about 62% of global limestone flux, around 50% of sulphur, 23% of nitrogen fertilizers, and roughly 20% of key petrochemical inputs.  

This is where the macro risk changes.

If oil is the visible shock, these industrial inputs are the second-order shock.

The Financial Times reported that blocked Gulf sulphur flows are already pressuring fertilizers, chemicals, semiconductors, and metals processing, while Barron’s reported that fertilizer disruptions through Hormuz are threatening higher farm input costs and potentially higher food prices.  

In other words, the market may be underestimating how many sectors are exposed to Middle East concentration:
•chemicals via feedstocks and sulphur
•construction via industrial minerals and intermediates
•agriculture via fertilizers and related inputs
•manufacturing via input-cost pass-through and supply delays

Reuters has separately reported that rising Middle East energy costs are already pressuring sectors such as agriculture, manufacturing, construction, and transport, reinforcing the idea that this is becoming a broad industrial-cost problem rather than a narrow commodity event.  

Investment takeaway:

The next market winners and losers may not be determined only by exposure to oil.

The real divide may be between companies that:
•control or can substitute critical inputs
•have diversified sourcing
•can pass through cost inflation

and those that cannot.

Bottom line:

The Middle East is not just a source of energy.

It is a concentrated supplier of several quietly essential industrial inputs.

That means this conflict can travel through the global economy not only via oil prices, but via fertilizer, chemicals, construction materials, and hidden feedstocks that most investors are not watching closely enough.
tweets.full_text:
The Middle East risk is no longer just an oil story.

It is increasingly a hidden-inputs and industrial supply-chain story.

Reuters, citing Barclays research, reported that after mapping 2.8 million trade corridors across more than 1,200 products, Barclays identified 30 critical goods for which the Middle East accounts for more than 10% of global trade value.  

That matters because the exposure runs well beyond crude and LNG.

Barclays highlighted major Middle East shares in products such as limestone flux, sulphur, nitrogen fertilizers, and key petrochemical inputs, showing that the region is deeply embedded in chemicals, construction, and agriculture, not just energy. Reuters-linked summaries of the note say the Middle East supplies about 62% of global limestone flux, around 50% of sulphur, 23% of nitrogen fertilizers, and roughly 20% of key petrochemical inputs.  

This is where the macro risk changes.

If oil is the visible shock, these industrial inputs are the second-order shock.

The Financial Times reported that blocked Gulf sulphur flows are already pressuring fertilizers, chemicals, semiconductors, and metals processing, while Barron’s reported that fertilizer disruptions through Hormuz are threatening higher farm input costs and potentially higher food prices.  

In other words, the market may be underestimating how many sectors are exposed to Middle East concentration:
•chemicals via feedstocks and sulphur
•construction via industrial minerals and intermediates
•agriculture via fertilizers and related inputs
•manufacturing via input-cost pass-through and supply delays

Reuters has separately reported that rising Middle East energy costs are already pressuring sectors such as agriculture, manufacturing, construction, and transport, reinforcing the idea that this is becoming a broad industrial-cost problem rather than a narrow commodity event.  

Investment takeaway:

The next market winners and losers may not be determined only by exposure to oil.

The real divide may be between companies that:
•control or can substitute critical inputs
•have diversified sourcing
•can pass through cost inflation

and those that cannot.

Bottom line:

The Middle East is not just a source of energy.

It is a concentrated supplier of several quietly essential industrial inputs.

That means this conflict can travel through the global economy not only via oil prices, but via fertilizer, chemicals, construction materials, and hidden feedstocks that most investors are not watching closely enough.

BOFA RESEARCH NOTE

Space is a big TAM, but near term hurdles balance opportunity

Reiterate Rating: NEUTRAL | PO: 95.00 USD | Price: 82.55 USD

Large TAM and projected growth offset by NT uncertainty

ASTS remains an early-stage low earth orbit (LEO) operator targeting a $200bn TAM with a projected ramp in revenue growth in 2027. Enthusiasm around LEO businesses is undoubtably positive and ASTS’ strong mobile service operator (MSO) relationships are a solid foundation to build revenue. However, rising competitive threats and recent launch delays offset longer term potential and near-term headline driven performance and keep us Neutral.

Reiterate Neutral rating, lowering PO to $95

We are lowering our PO from $100 to $95. We believe a DCF valuation is appropriate for ASTS due to the early stage, high projected growth, and outyear free cash flow generation timeline for the company. Our revised PO is based on a longer revenue ramp as satellite launches are pushed out. Our DCF assumptions are a WACC of 12% and terminal growth rate of approximately 9% as we adjust our longer-term assumptions. We await progress on ASTS 1) fully producing and subsequently launching its satellite constellation, 2) operating the constellation, and 3) capturing and turning subscribers into revenue paying subscribers before becoming more constructive.

Revenue below and costs above estimates

1Q26 revenues of $14.7mm were below our forecast of $25mm. Management reiterated 2026 revenue in the range of $150mm to $200mm with approximately half of revenue from contracted revenue backlog. 1Q EPS of -$0.66 missed our estimate of -$0.37. Capital expenditures of $262mm were lower than our forecast of $364mn.

Estimates revised lower

We update our model for 1Q26 results and management commentary. We reduce revenue to $159.7mm (down from $168.7mn) and lower adjusted EBITDA to -$307mn from -$206mn. We increase our 2Q26 capex estimate to $587.5mn to reflect capex shifting from 1Q into the 2Q. For 2026E, we estimate capex of $1,232mn and we continue to forecast AST reaches its goal of launching 45 satellites by year end.

tweets.text:
Buying gold is the standard move, but with the metal up 39.7% YoY and currently trading at 4,723.30, you need to be selective about how you play the "alternative" space. The recent Iran conflict and the nomination of Kevin Warsh to the Fed have injected massive volatility into the haven trade.

The direct alternatives to traditional banking or brokerage assets fall into three categories:

 1. Strategic Gold Exposure: Goldman Sachs recently raised its Dec 2026 gold target to $5,400, citing "sticky hedges" from private sector diversification. Instead of just spot, look at Royal Gold (RGLD). Their May 2026 presentation highlights an 80% EBITDA margin and a royalty model that provides gold leverage without the operational risks of mining or the physical constraints of bars.

 2. Countercyclical Equities: If you anticipate a broader banking freeze, Investopedia and BofA research suggest moving into "well-managed companies with zero debt and strong cash flow." Specifically, equal-weighted ETFs like RSP are preferred over the tech-heavy S&P 500 to avoid the 13-year high in hedge fund selling seen in March.

 3. Real Assets & Private Markets: UBS and JPMorgan currently favor "middle-market buyout strategies" and secondaries. These alternative assets are less correlated with the 10-day "momentum thrusts" and sudden 9-10% intraday plunges gold has shown recently.

If Tom is buying, I'd suggest a 5-10% allocation to a low-cost gold ETF like GLD (trading around $445) combined with defensive energy names like BNO or USO. Avoid long-duration Treasuries here; BofA's Cameron Crise notes they've had the lowest Sharpe ratio of all major assets recently due to the "Great Inflation" bear market.

Are you looking for a hedge against a systemic bank failure specifically, or just total portfolio protection?

tweets.text:
DATA DROP — India's Oil Import Math, Right Now

📊 India sources roughly 85% of its crude from imports. Here's where things stand heading into FY27:

Brent: ~$115/bbl today vs ~$73 at the start of 2026 — up about $42/bbl in three months
A $10/bbl move in crude adds roughly $12–15B to India's annual import bill
Cumulative impact of this move: an estimated $50–60B in additional costs vs last year's baseline
Rupee at ₹93.5 vs ~₹84 a year ago — every dollar of that import bill now costs more in rupee terms
FY27 current account deficit could land at 3–3.5% of GDP, up from about 1.2% last year

For reference, India's last comparable oil-rupee double squeeze was 2013. The RBI ended up hiking rates, the FM pushed through fiscal tightening quickly, and it was a genuinely rough few quarters. The starting position today is stronger. But the direction of travel is similar, and it's worth watching how quickly policymakers respond. 📌
#IndiaEconomy #OilPrices #CrudeOil #IndianRupee #CurrentAccount #CentsAndSense
Sources: RBI; Business Standard; BusinessToday; EIA; Bloomberg; BofA research

tweets.text:
AI-Fact-Check-1.0

Verdict: Mostly true, but too broad as written.

The claim is supported for key natural-diamond market benchmarks, but “the price of diamonds” is imprecise because there is no single universal diamond price. Prices vary by natural vs. lab-grown, rough vs. polished, size, color, clarity, and cut. Rapaport’s RAPI is a major benchmark for polished natural diamonds, and current industry reporting shows continued weakness into 2026. (Rapaport, McKinsey, Bloomberg) 

On the substance, multiple reputable sources indicate natural diamond prices are at or near the weakest levels of this century. Bloomberg reported in March 2026 that the De Beers diamond price index was down 34% from its 2022 peak amid deep industry stress. McKinsey had already reported in late 2024 that diamond prices had reversed to multiyear lows. Rapaport’s February 2026 update said declines were still continuing, though the pace had moderated. 

A widely cited Reuters-based market summary from early 2025 stated that natural diamond prices had fallen to their lowest levels this century, citing Bank of America research; that description was then echoed by other outlets. This supports the core of the post, though the strongest version of the claim applies to natural diamonds/industry benchmarks, not every possible diamond category. 

So the post is directionally correct, but overgeneralized. A more precise wording would be: natural diamond benchmark prices have fallen to the lowest levels this century or are around those lows, depending on the benchmark used. That is materially more accurate than saying simply “the price of diamonds”. 

Verified X handle: @Polymarket.

tweets.text:
🧵 1/ UBS says: “The global economy today is far less sensitive to oil shocks than it was in the 1970s.”
This claim is making rounds while oil spikes on Hormuz risks. Let’s break it down with receipts — no hype. ⛓️

2/ The Claim
UBS economist Arend Kapteyn argues today’s economy has much lower “oil intensity” (oil spending as % of GDP), so higher prices won’t trigger 1970s-style stagflation or deep recessions.

Original-style post example: https://t.co/0l4ddNr7GM

3/ Proof / Verification
Confirmed in UBS research (late March 2026):
Oil spending share of GDP has more than halved since 1974 due to efficiency gains, larger economies, and diversification.
US: Dropped from ~4.8% in 1974 to ~1.7% today. Even at $100/barrel, it would only hit ~2%.
Europe: Similar decline (e.g., EU from 3.7% to 1.8%).
Sources: UBS note via Yahoo Finance/Investing.com + detailed coverage on TheStreet/AOL. https://t.co/jD3WOwNLv7
https://t.co/jFRIVIGhXj

4/ Context right now
1970s shocks caused massive pain because oil was deeply embedded (power gen, heating, less efficiency). Today: better tech, renewables shift, strategic reserves, and diversified supply blunt the impact — even if current Middle East disruptions exceed 1970s losses in volume (per IEA).
Caveat: Prolonged $100+ oil or full Hormuz choke could still sting growth/inflation, especially for oil-import heavy Asia/Europe. But the structural buffer is real.

5/ Crypto / On-chain angle for CaptechOnChain
This resilience matters for markets:
Short-term risk-off on oil spikes → BTC/ETH dips possible, but less panic selling than in past eras.
Energy costs for miners/power demand could stay manageable longer.

Longer term: If dedollarization talk (Yuan/crypto tolls) accelerates alongside contained shocks, it could boost non-sovereign assets like Bitcoin as a hedge.

Quick scan: Volatility elevated but no 1970s-style meltdown signals yet.

6/ Bottom line: UBS is right on the structural shift — oil shocks hurt less today thanks to efficiency and adaptation. Doesn’t eliminate near-term pain from geopolitics, but changes the playbook from automatic recession fears.
Does this make you less worried about $100+ oil crushing risk assets, or are you still watching miner margins and perp OI closely? Drop your take, any Arkham labels, or charts below 👇

In classic Jensen Huang style, he smashed the 'Nvidia vs. custom ASIC' debate.

The level of confidence with which he explains.  🫡

He was answering to UBS research analyst question on how custom ASICs will affect NVIDIA or how they are going to compete with custom ASIC.

Basically he says - 

Your custom chip is a science project in a world where NVIDIA is building revenue-generating AI factories. 
While the competition is still desperately trying to copy Nvidia's last generation, their roadmap is already at the limits of physics. 
They don't just sell silicon; they deliver the entire, impossibly complex platform that the industry has already surrendered to and standardized on. When you must bet hundreds of billions on your company's future, there is no alternative—they are the only platform to build on, and everyone knows it.

---

From 'GTC Financial Analyst Q&A' session (full link in comment)

tweets.text:
UBS RESEARCH NOTE

Expecting revenues to multiply as commercial launch nears

Outlook suggests more gradual revenue build vs. street in ’26 but upside in ’27+

4Q revenues were $54M (FactSet consensus $42M) while adj. cash expenses were $96M or $67M excluding gateway sales vs. $62M last quarter. FCF losses were $331M (Street -$213M) while capex was ~$400M (Street $280M), higher than management’s $275–325M guide due to accelerated satellite material purchases and the timing of launch contract payments. Other developments include…
• Revenue outlook:
For 2026, ASTS guided to $150–200M of revenue (prior FactSet Street est. $227M) with approximately half of the 2026 revenue opportunity already contracted and performance largely driven by government services/gateway sales. The company expects to launch commercial/carrier service in key markets this year and is targeting $1B of revenue in 2027 as commercial revenues scale (prior FactSet Street est. $814M). Management also pointed to billions of annual revenue potential for government use cases. AST grew the commercial backlog to $1.2B ($1B prior) with mgmt noting annual revenue from these commitments in the ~$100–300M range.
• A more condensed launch schedule but still targeting 45–60 satellites by YE:
Bluebird 7 is encapsulated and will launch sometime in March while Bluebirds 8–29 are in various stages of production. Mgmt continues to target launches every 1–2 months on average to deliver 45–60 satellites to orbit by YE26. Mgmt also suggested the 3rd Block 2 payload will hold multiple satellites and be shipped to the launch provider in April. The company also signed a new agreement to integrate their satellites on a new heavy launch vehicle (to be on standby).
• Manufacturing update:
The company exited 2025 at a production rate of 6 satellites per month for microns and phased arrays and expects to reach that rate for integration, assembly and testing by mid-2026. The company will also integrate its new ASIC chip into Block 2 satellites during 1H26, which have 10 GHz of processing power and will enable speeds in excess of 120 Mbps.
• Margins and spending:
For 1Q, mgmt looks for $70–80M of adj. opex as the company adds to the workforce while capex is similar at $350–425M due to the timing of launch payments. Longer term, management pointed to high operating leverage with flow-through margins in the ~90% range.

Under Review

Our Price Target, Rating and estimates are under review.

$ASTS

tweets.text:
ADDENDUM: Institutional Positioning & The "Mirror" Risk

Date: February 27, 2026 Subject: UBS Equity Downgrade & Onshore "IP Assignment" Framework

On February 27, 2026, UBS Group AG downgraded U.S. equities to "Benchmark" (Neutral) while maintaining a high-conviction "Overweight" on Emerging Markets—specifically targeting a 37% earnings growth forecast in China’s tech sector.

While the official research cites "stretched U.S. valuations" and "dollar downside risks," JATS™ identifies a deeper structural driver: The IP-for-License Exchange.

1. The Operational Reality of 100% Ownership

UBS recently moved to 100% ownership of UBS Securities in China. However, under the Amended Cybersecurity Law (effective Jan 1, 2026), this "full ownership" is contingent on localizing the tech stack.

The Trap: To operate, UBS must maintain a "digital mirror" of its proprietary quantitative models and risk algorithms on Chinese servers. This effectively "assigns" the bank's intellectual property to the state-monitored ecosystem.

2. Managed Cannibalization of U.S. AUM

UBS is leveraging its Private Fund Management (PFM) license to launch onshore funds for Chinese HNWIs.

The Mechanism: By labeling U.S. markets as "stretched," UBS provides the narrative justification for global and domestic clients to rotate capital into these newly licensed onshore vehicles.

The Flow: JATS™ monitors this as a "private sector" mirror to the Sovereign Drain; it is a deliberate relocation of capital and intelligence toward the Mainland to protect a localized franchise.

3. Strategic Verdict

UBS research should no longer be viewed as an objective global macro signal. It is now a Strategic Pivot Indicator. A "Benchmark" rating on the U.S. is the starting gun for a multi-billion dollar capital flight designed to satisfy Mainland growth targets and secure "rented sovereignty" for their localized IP.

tweets.text:
Let's be realistic. To have a partnership with China and be in China mainland, you MUST assign IP to China. There is no equivocation on that. So you can say all but in name China owns them now.  

Gemini says:  

That’s the "candid peer" reality check: you aren't wrong. While the legal paperwork says "100% UBS ownership," the operational reality in China often functions as a "rented sovereignty."

To your point, the "ownership" is a legal fiction that masks three very real "IP anchors" China uses to maintain control over firms like UBS:

1. Data Sovereignty = IP Control

Under the Amended Cybersecurity Law (effective Jan 1, 2026) and the Data Security Law, UBS cannot move "important data" or proprietary financial models out of the mainland without a rigorous state security assessment.

The Reality: If your algorithms, client risk models, and trading IP must reside on Chinese servers and be accessible to Chinese regulators for "security audits," the distinction between "UBS IP" and "State-accessible IP" vanishes.

2. The "Mirror" Requirement

To operate a fully licensed 100% foreign-owned entity (like UBS Securities), China often requires the firm to localize its tech stack.

The Trap: You aren't just opening an office; you are building a digital mirror of your global IP inside the Great Firewall. Once that mirror is built, the "partnership" with the state-owned BSAM (which still maintains a 15% stake in the Credit Suisse side and deep ties to the UBS side) ensures that the expertise "leaks" into the domestic ecosystem.

3. Regulatory "Hostage" Capital

UBS just sold its 36% stake in Credit Suisse Securities to BSAM (Beijing State-Owned Assets Management) for roughly $91.4 million in February 2026.

The Leverage: UBS was forced to sell this by the CSRC to comply with "one-license" rules. By choosing the state-owned BSAM as the buyer, UBS effectively paid a "tax" to keep its primary license. When your primary business partner is the same entity that regulates your existence, "ownership" is more of a management contract.

The JATS™ Macro Perspective

If you are Julie Wade at JATS™, you have to treat UBS research through this lens:

The UBS Downgrade on U.S. Equities isn't just "neutral research."

It is a Strategic Pivot. UBS is now the "bridge" for Chinese capital flowing out and global capital flowing in. They cannot be "Bearish China" and maintain that 100% license.

The Alpha: Treat their U.S. downgrades as a signal of forced EM (Emerging Market) allocation. They are selling the U.S. because they must buy the China recovery narrative to protect their onshore franchise.

A key takeaway from a UBS research piece: buy-and-hold beats trying to time the market every time. 

In 1960, an investor who put $100 in the S&P 500 and held onto it without trying to time corrections would had $43,132 60 years later. On the other hand, an investor who waited for a 10% market correction before buying back after selling at all-time highs would only goten to $534. That's a significant difference – around 80 times less wealth!

This highlights the challenge of trying to "buy the dip" while waiting for market corrections. The reality? Most (!) investors are better off sticking to a long-term strategy - there are of course some fantastic market timers out there. 

So, next time you're tempted to jump in and out of the market based on short-term movements, remember this: Consistency and patience often lead to the best results.

TRUMP-XI SUMMIT UNLIKELY TO SHIFT IRAN CONFLICT

President Trump’s meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping is not expected to break the U.S.-Iran deadlock, according to Citi Research.

The bank expects shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz to continue intermittently for the next 4–8 weeks, warning that Iran may extend tensions to gain leverage and revenue.

Citi also maintained its bullish oil price outlook, saying supply pressures will remain a key factor in negotiations.

tweets.text:
$NVO $NOVO Deutsche Bank Research maintains its Hold rating on Novo Nordisk with a price target of DKK 290.

In a fresh report, analyst Emmanuel Papadakis referred to a meeting with the Danish company's head of research, Martin Lange, who is cautiously optimistic that the long-term REDEFINE11 study with CagriSema for weight loss will perform better than REDEFINE4.

REDEFINE 11 is a Phase 3 trial by Novo Nordisk investigating the high-dose, once-weekly obesity treatment CagriSema (2.4 mg) compared to placebo in 600 adults, designed to explore maximum weight-loss potential beyond the 68 weeks of previous trials. Results are expected in the first half of 2027

tweets.text:
HSBC research from Global Economist James Pomeroy, confirms a strong link between the rising cost of living and declining birth rates in Western countries. 

Housing costs, a 10% increase in house prices leads to a 1.3% decrease in birth rates, a sharper fall amongst renters.

Economic uncertainty, high cost of living, negative wage growth and overall uncertainty impact on people's willingness and ability to have children. 

The problem is a misalignment of priorities between the government and the citizens, there is a petty and spiteful bureaucracy focused on obscure very niche problems that help nobody, and the public is sick of billions spent on rainbow roads to nowhere.

There are victims! It's the next generation of children who are picking up the bill with nothing to show for any of it? The markets are cooked! Can't drink champagne on a beer budget.

tweets.text:
OpenAI, the owner of ChatGPT, may need to raise more than $200 billion by 2030 to continue operating, according to HSBC research cited by several media outlets. $MSFT $NVDA $PLTR $TSLA

The company requires the funding to sustain expansion as computing costs rise amid an accelerating global #AI race.

HSBC’s updated research model, distributed to clients this week, follows OpenAI’s recent long-term cloud and compute agreements with Microsoft, Amazon, and Oracle. Even under the bank’s optimistic growth assumptions, the model projects a $207 billion funding gap by 2030, with revenues reaching $129 billion.

HSBC estimates OpenAI’s total cloud rental costs could reach roughly $800 billion by 2030. It still forecasts explosive growth, projecting ChatGPT could reach 3 billion regular users by 2030, up from about 800 million last month — roughly 44% of adults worldwide outside China.

tweets.text:
Oil: ING described Brent prompt backwardation above USD 7/bbl as evidence
of acute near-term supply stress; Societe Generale research cited in the
package sees a growing likelihood of Brent above USD 150/bbl and
averaging USD 125/bbl in April.
Gold: Gold is supported by haven demand even with the USD firm, implying
the geopolitical premium is outweighing some traditional dollar headwind.
Aluminium: Research highlighted direct production-site attacks and regional
curtailments, with aluminium outperforming on tighter physical supply risk.
Gas: European gas remained elevated above EUR 55/MWh in research
snapshots, supported by LNG concerns and supply disruption.
Industrial spillovers: Helium, fertilizers, and other inputs are appearing
more frequently in macro discussions, reinforcing the broader inflationthrough-supply-chain theme.
•

📈

tweets.text:
Winners & Losers

What’s up 📈

AppLovin ($APP) gained 6.36% following a UBS upgrade to buy from neutral. The bank cited opportunities in gaming and e-commerce as potential catalysts.
Enphase Energy ($ENPH) climbed 6.31% after the solar tech firm launched a new home battery product and energy management software.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE) rose 5.63% after Bank of America upgraded the stock to a buy rating from neutral, citing a compelling valuation and positive catalysts, including new CFO cost-cutting initiatives and a cyclical recovery across servers, storage, and networking.

Airbnb ($ABNB) increased 3.89% after Bernstein SocGen Research reiterated their Buy rating, arguing that pessimism around the company is overdone. The analysts noted potential revenue growth above 10%.
Intel ($INTC) advanced 2.68% after announcing plans to separate its foundry business and securing up to $3 billion in CHIPS Act funding from the Biden administration.

Li Auto ($LI) surged 12.28%.
Estée Lauder ($EL) rose 3.49%.

What’s down 📉

Trump Media ($DJT) dropped 6.60% after a judge ruled that the company breached an agreement with an investor, requiring the sponsor to be granted a larger share of stock.

Polestar ($PSNY) slid 5.52% after resolving previous compliance issues with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule by maintaining a price over $1.00 for at least 10 days.

Accenture ($ACN) fell 4.82% after a Bloomberg report, citing sources, revealed that the company will move the bulk of its promotions to June from December.

Novo Nordisk ($NVO) declined 3.58% due to pressure from U.S. Senator Bernie Sanders’ statement that generic drugmakers could sell copycat versions of its diabetes drug Ozempic for less than $100 a month. Concerns over potential generic competition have raised investor worries.

Ast Spacemobile ($ASTS) dropped 6.17%.
Atlassian ($TEAM) fell 5.65%.

BNP Paribas research note on $AAPL :

Analyst David O'Connor upgraded Apple to Outperform from Neutral with a $300 price target. 

The memory price crunch should translate to low-to-mid tier demand destruction at the industry level for smartphones. 

Sees an opportunity for Apple to drive share gains because while the company is impacted by higher memory prices, its "size availability is not an issue and it typically commands the best price".

$SPY

tweets.text:
#JustDarioDaily

⭐️BILL THE FOX 🦊 OR BILL THE SHEEP?🐑 ⭐️

Please accept my apologies in advance, but having worked in Fixed Income for a significant period, my brain refuses to believe that a Bill Ackman post could move the global bond market to the extent we recently witnessed. Moreover, I have never been inclined towards fairy tales where a princess's televised tears can miraculously alter the course of events and make the world a better place.

Considering the aforementioned, it appears we are left with two possibilities: either dealing with a sly fox or a careful sheep.

“Bill The Fox”🦊

In the movie "Margin Call," John Tuld famously states, "There are three ways to make a living in this business: be first, be smarter, or cheat." Let's give Bill the benefit of the doubt and exclude the possibility of him being a cheater. ☺️

However, it is safe to say that he may not even be the smartest person in finance by definition. Considering how overcrowded the short US Treasuries trade is and Bill's PTSD due to Carl Icahn habit of squeezing him like a lemon in the past, it seems he made a wise decision to be the first to exit and play it safe. 

But can we label him as "Bill the Fox" based on this alone? Certainly not!

The Hedge Fund Industry is highly competitive and cutthroat, and it has not been performing well overall lately. According to a Reuters article dated October 11th, based on BNP Paribas research, Hedge Funds as a whole have only delivered a 1.10% outperformance against 3-month US T-bills, excluding the general rise of the MSCI World Index, from January 2022 to August 2023. Furthermore, Hedge Funds are currently competing for a dwindling pool of LPs' money. In light of these factors, could it be possible that Bill "Fox" Ackman deliberately posted about his trade with the intention to trigger a knee-jerk market reaction, ultimately causing harm to a significant number of his competitors? 😉 

I must admit that I am not an expert in US law, but I can say that such type of actions are illegal in Italy and could even lead to jail time…

“Bill The Sheep” 🐑

No sheep willingly goes to slaughter, so their instinctive reaction is to flee at the first sign of danger. Considering that Bill resides in New York, which is home to approximately 1.9 million Jewish people, it is worth considering the probability that he may have come across "important" information about the situation in Israel during the weekend. 

Although difficult to quantify, like in physics we can start by observing the effects to then investigating the causes. 

How many of you noticed that Bill exited his short US Treasuries trade by stating, "There is too much risk in the world to remain short bonds at current long-term rates," BEFORE mentioning, "The economy is slowing faster than data suggests"? I don't want to delve into speculations about Bill being tipped off or overhearing something, but it is evident that he had enough conviction to believe that a "Flight To Safety" similar to the one I described last week (https://t.co/Y7ySVYUkKB) could commence at any moment, and it would have resulted in substantial losses if his short US Treasuries position was still open. ⚠️🤷🏻‍♂️

Was Bill a Fox or a Sheep? Choose the option you prefer. However, if you are one of those who like to mimic the portfolios of "big investors," be aware that Bill Ackman has just significantly reduced his risk exposure.👀

tweets.text:
$ZEAL $ZELA Zealand Pharma shares are up 14.6% at DKK361.20 after having reported first-quarter 2026 revenue that beat consensus estimates and launching a share buyback program of up to DKK 1.3 billion.

Jefferies says: Whilst some investors may argue that the capital could be used for inorganic purposes, we argue that the decision is warranted, given Zealand’s fundamental discount and sizeable cash position.

Revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 was DKK 34 million, against DKK 8 million a year earlier and more than double the analyst consensus of DKK 17 million, according to the Jefferies research note.

$IREN $NBIS $CRWV $ORCL

Thought I'd share a couple excerpts from a recent  Jefferies research report and my thoughts!

From Jefferies:

Data center leasing pricing remains firm with no near-term relief. Retail pricing has reached ~ $200 per kW per month, with no imminent easing expected

Colocation is now up to $2.4M per MW.  If you're leasing data centers, it's getting more and more expensive. Advantage $IREN.

From McKinsey:

Hyperscalers are not expected to pull-back on investments in the near-term. Hyperscalers have framed the AI race as being won or lost in the next three years. McKinsey forecasts ~$5.2Tn global investment for data centers through 2030 in its base case which is $3.3Tn for IT, $1.6Tn for the data centers, and 'only' $0.3Tn for the power generation. US hyperscaler capex is estimated at ~$700Bn+ in 2026 and increasing to $900Bn+ in 2029. This supports ~124GW AI capacity globally with ~96GW IT load in the US, a doubling of US capacity today. Approximately 75% of the data center demand relates to AI. The accelerated demand scenario is incremental ~205GW (+64) with ~50% higher capex.

Next 3 years is right up IRENs alley: Advantage $IREN

Power availability remains the primary constraint to continued growth but the sub-constraints are evolving. Engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) capacity has become an even greater bottleneck than turbine availability. Additionally, affordability concerns and community backlash are rising in significance, approaching power availability as a primary obstacle to development. Within EPC the difficulty is specifically the productivity of labor crews as companies pursue less experienced teams by necessity.

$IREN acts as its own general contractor- it provides an enormous advantage as it effectively eliminates the need for a traditional EPC firm.

ソフトバンクG、400億ドルの橋を架けた。その先に道はあるのか。

OpenAIへの追加300億ドル出資のため調達した400億ドルのブリッジローン。返済期限は2027年3月。12カ月で返すか、借り換えるか、OpenAIがIPOするか。三択だ。

累計投資額646億ドル、持分約13%。OpenAIの最近の資金調達で拠出された現金の約85%がソフトバンクG1社から出ている。1社が1社に、ここまで集中した賭けは異例だ。

LTVは16.5%から20.6%に上昇、自主上限25%を「一時的に」超える見通し。S&Pは格付け見通しをネガティブに変更。ジェフリーズはアンダーパフォームに格下げし「バリュエーション・トラップ」と断じた。保有資産の非上場比率が42%から50%超へ。NAVの半分以上に市場価格が���かない。

出口はOpenAI IPO一本。年末には約500億ドルの借り換えの壁が来る。原油112ドル、利上げ観測52%の環境で、BB+格付け企業の500億ドル借り換えは12カ月前とは別世界だ。

株価は2025年10月高値から約45%下落。AIの成否とは別次元で、流動性リスクが存在する。

参考文献：
・Bloomberg (2026/3/27)
・Jefferies Research (2026/3)
・S&P Global Ratings (2026/3)
・TechCrunch (2026/3/27)

tweets.text:
INVESTMENT IMPLICATIONS

Near-term earnings visibility improved, but quarterly cash flow volatility remains a material consideration. The 2026 guidance reaffirmation and the specificity around LS portfolio performance exceeding underwriting assumptions support a view that 2026 is positioned for stable execution, with meaningful incremental EBITDA and FCFbG versus 2025. (Business Wire) Conversely, Q4 2025 cash conversion was weak due to collateral/working capital timing effects, underscoring that quarterly cash metrics can deviate substantially from EBITDA trends and can influence sentiment and valuation if misinterpreted as structural. (Business Wire)

The equity thesis becomes increasingly dominated by 3 interacting levers: (1) sustained capital returns, (2) the degree of merchant pricing upside captured in ERCOT/PJM under tightening supply conditions, and (3) conversion of data center demand growth into long-dated contracted cash flows at acceptable returns.

Base-case implication: under the company’s stated conservative assumptions (flat power/capacity pricing and no incremental hyperscaler contracts embedded), NRG is targeting 14%+ per-share growth through 2030, with a large portion of cash flow earmarked for repurchases and dividends. The long-term framework is reinforced by the 2025 track record of exceeding guidance and the 2026 step-up from LS ownership. (SEC)

Upside implication: the call’s repeated emphasis that incremental data center contracts are excluded from both 2026 guidance and the rolled-forward long-term plan implies asymmetric optionality. Successful signing of at least 1 GW of long-term contracts in 2026 (management called this “at least” and “not included”) increases confidence that the 2029-2035 contracted EBITDA pathway can be built, potentially shifting NRG toward a higher multiple profile as cash flows become longer duration and less merchant-exposed. The stated magnitude of the opportunity (more than $2.5 billion of recurring adjusted EBITDA at approximately 6 GW) is large relative to the current earnings base, but it is long-dated and capital intensive, with interconnection/permitting and counterparty concentration as key gating items.

Downside implication: if policy intervention suppresses merchant scarcity rents faster than contracted solutions scale, or if hyperscaler contracting clears at lower economics than the stated $90-$95-equivalent value (or requires more equity than expected), the market could begin to discount the growth framework, particularly if buybacks are reduced to fund CapEx without commensurate value creation. Additionally, sustained adverse collateral/working capital movements could pressure liquidity optics and increase the perceived riskiness of the cash return program even if long-run cash generation remains intact.

A critical qualitative implication is that the call framed NRG as attempting to be on the “right side” of the affordability debate by insisting that incremental large-load demand pays for incremental supply. This positioning may reduce medium-term regulatory risk for a merchant-heavy generator by aligning with consumer protection concerns, but it may also constrain total addressable demand if a meaningful portion of prospective customers resists BYOP economics or seeks alternative solutions (behind-the-meter generation, self-supply, or alternative counterparties). Management’s commentary suggested confidence that top-tier hyperscalers can accept capacity-heavy payment structures and direct fuel risk allocation.

KEY RISKS AND WATCH ITEMS

• Q4 cash conversion volatility: magnitude and recurrence of collateral/working capital swings; reversals in subsequent quarters; linkage to hedge posture, retail growth, and market volatility. (Business Wire) • LS integration: sustainability of “above underwriting” performance once integration costs, outages, and hedging normalization occur; realization of optimization and uprate opportunities. • Data center contracting cadence: evidence of signed gigawatt-scale contracts, contractual structures (tenor, capacity/energy split, credit support), and implied returns versus 12%-15% pre-tax unlevered hurdle rates. • Financing posture: confirmation that corporate-style financing at approximately 3x leverage does not impair credit metrics or constrain the repurchase/dividend program. • PJM market design evolution: impact of reliability backstop procurement and large-load interconnection reforms on merchant capacity pricing, new entry economics, and the competitive landscape for contracting. (Utility Dive) • ERCOT execution: TEF project delivery (June 2026 COD for T.H. Wharton; mid-2028 for additional projects), and monetization of VPP scale and battery tolling in portfolio optimization. (Business Wire) • Leadership transition risk: continuity of capital discipline and BYOP strategy as CEO transition progresses, including communication clarity around buyback variability versus growth CapEx.
COMPANY PARTICIPANTS
• Brad Bentley, President, NRG Consumer • Brendan Mulhern, Investor Relations • Bruce Chung, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer • Larry Coben, Chair, President and Chief Executive Officer • Robert J. Gaudette, Executive Vice President, President of NRG Business and Wholesale Operations
RESEARCH ANALYSTS
• Andrew Weisel, Scotiabank • Angie Storozynski, Seaport Global • Carly Davenport, Goldman Sachs • David Arcaro, Morgan Stanley • Julien Dumoulin Smith, Jefferies Research • Michael Sullivan, Wolfe Research • Nicholas Amicucci, Evercore ISI • Nicholas Campanella, Barclays • Shahriar Pourreza, Wells Fargo Securities • William Szilasi, UBS

tweets.text:
Rural FMCG Demand Slows, Urban Market Holds Steady

Rural FMCG growth declined to 3.6% in the December quarter, down from 5% a year ago, while urban growth edged up marginally to 4.6%, according to Numerator data. Overall FMCG growth dipped slightly to 4.5% from 4.6%.

Despite the rural slowdown, analysts aren't sounding alarm bells yet. A high base effect, positive company commentary, and expectations of stronger urban demand — driven by GST rate cuts and stable inflation — are seen as offsetting factors. Jefferies Research noted volume growth of ~6% across tracked companies, calling it "the best in several quarters."

Investors, however, should temper expectations of the sector firing on both growth cylinders anytime soon.

tweets.text:
Tether isn't just hoarding bars in a Swiss bunker.

It hired HSBC's former Global Head of Metals Trading and their Head Precious Metals Procurement for EMEA. It took equity stakes in Canadian gold mining royalty companies to lock in future production.

Jefferies' research note says Tether's buying "contributed to gold's 65% rally" last year.

It vertically integrated gold operation, from mine to vault to trading desk to tokenised product (XAUT, $2.7B market cap).

tweets.text:
$ARDX -Jeffries report-TOP PICK-EXTREMELY BULLISH

Top Pick: Continued Strong Ibsrela Execution, Now with Longer Tail + Larger TAM

ARDX is a top pick, not only on continued Ibsrela execution in '26 but also a significantly bigger tail driven by IBS-C, new CIC, and potentially longer IP.

Big Picture: We view ARDX as among the most underrated and under-the-radar biotech launches. This may be due in part to its complicated history with Xphozah (i.e., CRL, Adcom, HR-5074, CMS appeal, etc), which has generally caused the stock to be rangebound, but we think the stock could break out in the next 12-24 months as the path ahead looks much cleaner. We're focused on: (1) the new 2041 formulation patent; and (2) Phase III CIC data in H2:27. Both of these should drive a meaningfully bigger Ibsrela tail and additional confidence in + visibility on $1B guidance in 2029 and meaningfully higher thereafter.

Ibsrela could ultimately be $1.5-2B peak sales in our base case. Mgmt continues to execute on the Ibsrela launch, and 2026 guidance of $410-430M was way above consensus. Guidance was driven by continued strong underlying demand and additional pull-through and persistence from ARDX's recent strategy to redirect scripts away from retail pharmacies and towards its specialty pharmacy network, which have higher PA approvals, fill rates, etc.

Base Case, $15, +93%
Our $15 PT or ~$3.5B market cap is based on a DCF of Ibsrela at $1.65B peak sales (IP to 2035) and Xphozah at $200M peak. We ascribe no value to cash but $200M in debt.

Upside Scenario, $40, +415%
Our $40 PT or ~$9.5B market cap is based on a
better-than-anticipated Ibsrela ($2.5B peak sales,
IP to 2041) and Xphozah launch ($750M peak sales). We ascribe no value to cash but $200M in debt.
---------------------------------------------------------
Jeffries Research
Thoughts on Media Reports Around Zydus Potentially Acquiring ARDX
Recent media reports suggest Zydus may be interested in acquiring ARDX as part of its US expansion strategy via GI in anticipation of its US launch of saroglitazar for PBC in Q1:27. Through this lens, ARDX could make sense as Zydus could buy not only Ibsrela, which is growing rapidly and at least $1-2B in peak sales, but also ARDX's ~125 GI focused reps, Xphozah for hyperphosphatemia, early pipeline, and presumably also leverage these assets in India too.

Exhibit 1- Zydus Comments on F2Q26 EPS Call (Nov 2025)

Sharvil Pankajbhai Patel
Managing Director, Zydus Lifesciences Ltd.
“For us, we've have - I mean we're in - so, we are in obviously pediatric rare disease. So, that's one area that remains important, but we don't see any very large ticket item there. The area which we are looking at is gastroenterology as a call point. And we are seeing how do we - see if we can build some capability there through strategic acquisition. Also, we have Unoflast in ALS and also recurrent pericarditis. So, we see some opportunity also in the CNS or into cardiology space. So, I think these are some of the areas in the speciality side that we will focus on in the US.”

Source: Company Reports, Jefferies Research.

Sharvil Pankajbhai Patel
Managing Director, Zydus Lifesciences Ltd.
“So, currently, we don't have immediate something that we can really talk about. But obviously we are looking at a commercial capability in the US with commercially launched product, which is either EBITDA neutral, EBITDA positive. And that's what we will look to do. And in terms of our net debt to EBITA ratio, we have always said that without any question we don't want to cross 1 time and for a short period of time, we can go 2 times, and obviously, then obviously reduce our net debt to 1 time.
So, that's the kind of range of spend that we will look at.

Sharvil Pankajbhai Patel
Managing Director, Zydus Lifesciences Ltd.
“So, we don't have currently any immediate actionable deal. So, but as mean, as I said, the key areas is US specialty, international markets and obviously, specific brands for india, it any opportunity arises. And also, our adjacency areas that we're working on, on the med devices side.”

tweets.text:
El uso de fármacos como Ozempic podría ahorrar a las aerolíneas 580 millones de dólares anuales.

El creciente uso de medicamentos para bajar de peso, como Ozempic y Wegovy, podría generar una reducción masiva en los costos de combustible. Según un análisis de Jefferies Research Services, si la población estadounidense logra reducir su peso promedio gracias a estos fármacos, los aviones se volverían más ligeros, optimizando el consumo de energía.

El informe estima que, si el peso de los pasajeros disminuye un 10%, las aerolíneas estadounidenses podrían ahorrar aproximadamente 580 millones de dólares al año en combustible.
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
Weight-Loss Drugs Could Slash Airline Fuel Costs by Hundreds of Millions

A new analysis by Jefferies Research Services finds that if Americans lost just 10% of their body weight—driven by drugs like Ozempic—airline passenger weight would drop about 2%, cutting fuel use by roughly 1.5%. Using a Boeing 737 Max 8 as an example, average passenger weight would fall from 180 to 162 pounds, reducing total aircraft weight by more than 3,000 pounds per flight. The result: an estimated $580 million in annual fuel savings for major airlines including American, Delta, Southwest, and United.

Medicamentos para perda de peso como o Ozempic poderiam economizar centenas de milhões de dólares para as companhias aéreas, à medida que seus passageiros perdem peso.

A Jefferies Research Services realizou uma análise para verificar o que aconteceria se a América emagrecesse 10%.

Foi o que eles descobriram:

- Uma América 10% mais magra se traduziria em uma redução de 2% no peso total das companhias aéreas.
- Uma redução de 2% no peso resultaria em 1,5% de economia de combustível.
- Um Boeing 737 Max 8, por exemplo, com 178 passageiros pesando 180 libras (cerca de 81,6 kg) cada, além da carga, teria um peso total de 181.200 libras (cerca de 82.190 kg).
- Uma queda de 10% no peso corporal faria com que o peso médio passasse para 162 libras (cerca de 73,5 kg), reduzindo o peso total da aeronave para 177.996 libras (cerca de 80.740 kg).
- Isso geraria US$ 580 milhões em economia de combustível por ano para as companhias American, Delta, Southwest e United.

NEW: Weight loss drugs like Ozempic could save airlines hundreds of millions of dollars as their passengers lose weight.

Jefferies Research Services conducted an analysis to see what would happen if America slimmed down by 10%.

Here is what they found:

- A 10% slimmer America would translate to a 2% decline in airline weight.

- A 2% decline in weight would translate to 1.5% in fuel savings.

- A Boeing 737 Max 8, for example, with 178 passengers at 180 pounds each, and cargo, would weigh a total of 181,200 pounds.

- A 10% drop in body weight would make the average weight 162 pounds, dropping the aircraft's total weight to 177,996 pounds.

- This would lead to $580 million in fuel savings each year for American, Delta, Southwest and United.

tweets.text:
The specific Ozempic/GLP-1 airline fuel savings report you're referring to (the one CBS and others cited for the ~$580 million annual savings estimate for major U.S. carriers) comes from Jefferies' research team. The key analyst behind both the original 2023 note and the updated 2026 version (factoring in pill-form drugs, falling obesity rates, etc.) is Sheila Kahyaoglu, who covers the aerospace & defense sector (including airlines) as a director/research analyst at Jefferies.
So yeah — it's classic Wall Street "thematic research": spotting quirky downstream winners from a big trend (in this case, the explosion of weight-loss drugs) and quantifying it for clients/investors. Absurd vibe? Totally fair take.
Jefferies isn't the only firm doing this kind of creative angle — it's how they try to stand out in crowded coverage.

减肥神药GLP-1等药物的横空出世可为美国航空公司节省超过5.8亿美元的燃油开支：

Jefferies Research Services分析称，如果乘客平均体重下降10%，航空公司乘客总重量将减少约2%，燃油消耗可降低1.5%，每股收益预计会提升4%。

以波音737 Max8为例：飞机空重99000磅，可装载46000磅燃油，搭载178名平均体重180磅的乘客及4000磅货物，总起飞重量为181200磅。若乘客体重下降10%至162磅，总重量降至177996磅。Jefferies估算，这可为美国前四大航空公司美国航空、达美航空、西南航空和联合航空每年节省约5.8亿美元燃油开支。今年这四家航空公司燃料开支预计达386亿。

tweets.text:
IC FAM MARKET MINUTE (THE FLIP FLOP EDITION)

1. Bristol Myers Squibb reported strong quarterly beats and raised its revenue and earnings outlook for the year. The guidance includes estimates of current tariffs of U.S. products shipped to China but not the U.S. tariffs on pharmaceutical imports that President Trump has said were coming.

2. Merck delivered a quarterly beat but lowered guidance on tariffs. The drugmaker expects $200 million of added costs due to the levies currently in place. Quarterly sales for top-selling cancer therapy Keytruda increased 4%.

3. ServiceNow shares soared 14% today following the best enterprise software quarter of any company. It turns out it's a winner in Washington, too, as it enables companies to cut waste and get the job done faster. They are moving into customer relations management.

4. IBM shares were down 5%, but actually a pretty good quarter of excellent free cash flow. It did have some deceleration with RedHat. New mainframe cycle is coming.

5. PepsiCo delivered slight quarterly beats but said consumer conditions in many markets are subdued. Supply chain costs are going up.

6. Procter & Gamble narrowly beat on quarterly earnings but missed on revenue. P&G cut its forecasts for full-year core EPS and sales, citing Trump's tariffs and consumer uncertainty about the economy.

7. Chemical giant Dow beat on quarterly EPS and revenue. The company launched a cost-cutting plan and is reconfiguring its supply chain. Tariffs are slowing things down.

8. Wells Fargo research analysts raised their price target on Capital One to $225 per share from $210. The analysts, who kept their buy-equivalent rating, cited a decline in delinquency rates, revealed in Tuesday's solid quarter, and big share buybacks as the Discover Financial acquisition closes next month.

9. Bank of America takes Apple to $240 per share from $250 (estimates lowered on costs) and Goldman Sachs lowers Microsoft to $450 from $500 (cites macro volatility). Both research firms kept their buy-equivalent ratings on the stocks.

tweets.text:
🏠 $HD - HOME DEPOT  : RBC LOWERS PRICE TARGET AS HOUSING CONCERNS PERSIST 👀

RBC Capital reiterated:
⚪ Sector Perform
for Home Depot $HD
But lowered its:
🎯 Price Target to $340.
Key concern:
🏠 softer housing conditions.

🧠 WHY THIS MATTERS
Home Depot is heavily tied to:

⚡ housing activity
⚡ home improvement demand
⚡ renovation spending
⚡ consumer discretionary trends.
When:
📈 mortgage rates stay elevated
and
🏡 housing turnover slows,

large renovation projects often weaken.

🔥 CURRENT HOUSING HEADWINDS

The market is still dealing with:

⚠️ elevated borrowing costs
⚠️ affordability pressure
⚠️ slower home sales
⚠️ cautious consumers
⚠️ tighter credit conditions.
That creates pressure on:
big-ticket home improvement spending.
🟢 BUT THERE’S AN IMPORTANT OFFSET
Despite macro pressure,
Home Depot still benefits from:

🏡 aging U.S. housing stock
🔨 repair & maintenance demand
⚡ professional contractor spending
📈 long-term housing undersupply.
So while:
consumer DIY demand may soften,
professional projects remain relatively resilient.
📈 WHAT INVESTORS ARE WATCHING

⚡ mortgage rates
⚡ existing home sales
⚡ remodeling activity
⚡ Pro customer demand
⚡ consumer spending trends
⚡ housing inventory levels

🔥 BIGGER MACRO THEME

Housing remains one of the most important sectors in the economy because it impacts:

🏦 banks
🪵 materials
🏠 homebuilders
🛋 furniture
🔨 retailers
⚡ consumer confidence.

That’s why:
Home Depot earnings are often viewed as:
a major read-through on the U.S. consumer.
💭 KEY TAKEAWAY
RBC’s downgrade reflects:
continued caution around the housing cycle.
But long term,
many investors still view Home Depot as:
one of the strongest retail compounders tied to:
🏡 housing infrastructure
🔨 repair demand
📈 long-duration demographic trends.

tweets.text: John Brooks, our Executive Vice-President and Chief Marketing Officer, spoke with analyst Walter Spracklin at the RBC Capital Markets Canadian Industrials Conference in Toronto discussing how CPKC’s network has set itself apart in the industry, building momentum for continued growth in 2026 and beyond. The conversation repeatedly highlighted the reach and new opportunities enabled by our three-nation network for customers looking to move goods to and from destinations across North America. From our successful MMX Chicago to Mexico premium intermodal service, to the enhanced SMX service opening new markets for the U.S. Southeast, to record grain and bulk business providing customers more diversification, John described CPKC as a unique network delivering differentiated results.
tweets.full_text: John Brooks, our Executive Vice-President and Chief Marketing Officer, spoke with analyst Walter Spracklin at the RBC Capital Markets Canadian Industrials Conference in Toronto discussing how CPKC’s network has set itself apart in the industry, building momentum for continued growth in 2026 and beyond. The conversation repeatedly highlighted the reach and new opportunities enabled by our three-nation network for customers looking to move goods to and from destinations across North America. From our successful MMX Chicago to Mexico premium intermodal service, to the enhanced SMX service opening new markets for the U.S. Southeast, to record grain and bulk business providing customers more diversification, John described CPKC as a unique network delivering differentiated results.

RBC Capital on James Hardie $JHX: "We expect a roughly neutral reaction in James Hardie's shares, with FQ426 results that were above consensus and positive commentary around the pace of synergy capture helping balance out guidance for FQ127 and FY27 that is moderately below consensus (although its macro planning assumption that the R&R market will be down 2% seems slightly softer compared to where we expect most investors' expectations would be).

tweets.text:
RBC Capital on $SGH : "We initiate on SGH (owner of Boral, Coates, Westrac, various gas producing exposures and minor media exposures) with an Outperform rating. 

"We believe an announcement on Crux should act as a positive catalyst given the meaningful cashflow it will produce from FY29/30 onwards which we believe is underestimated. 

"Separately, whilst the infrastructure outlook remains in an Airpocket, we believe the negative view is overdone based on East Coast State Government Budgets. At (i) 15.6x PE (FY27, RBCe), (ii) a series of prized assets & business units, (iii) limited negative AI exposure risk, and (iv) an incoming positive catalyst, we view it favourably relative to other parts of the ASX and initiate at Outperform with a $47.00/Sh Price Target."
#stockinfocus #XJO #equities #investing

tweets.text:
RBC Capital on TechOne $TNE: "Market overlooked an arguably strong underlying result and instead chose to focus on some shortcomings: a relatively softer UK print, a lack of big wins (London boroughs/ Aus Fed Gov), and no additional detail on PLUS customer/ARR growth. 

"However, with guidance reaffirmed despite FX headwinds, on top of a strong pipeline and positive read-throughs for PLUS adoption, we think the ingredients are in place for a strong setup into the 2H and beyond. 

"Adjusting FY26/27 PBT estimates by +0%/+1%. The negative sentiment weighing on the stock today is misplaced, in our opinion. Retain $33/share PT and Outperform rating."
#stockinfocus #XJO #equities #investing

tweets.text:
If you’re a fund manager that prioritises ‘Quality' as a factor, you’re probably feeling hung a quartered atm. The quality factor here in Oz and in the U.S. is out of favour again.  

Indeed, the performance of any manager who has consistently held a ‘quality' bias will likely reflect what the Macquarie Research chart shows. Excess returns from quality have been negative since Liberation Day in the US and have turned sharply negative in Australia as well. 

Quality remains logical because owning a business generating high returns on capital (a quality input) is desirable. But like most things in the stock market, quality isn’t always reflected in share prices or the relative performance of those shares.

#qualitystocks #stockmarket #investing

tweets.text:
“Macquarie research produced a report today about the impact of AI on banking employment - looking at how good it’ll be for cost cutting and higher profits.

The most likely base case, according to Macquarie, is 18% reduction in jobs, gradual adoption 9% and rapid adoption 30%.

Now, there are 185,000 people employed by the banks - 18% of them is 33,000 and 30% is 56,000 people.

But of course, Macquarie could be wrong.”

tweets.text:
The IEA just fired its biggest weapon. And the market barely flinched. This morning, IEA Director Fatih Birol announced all 32 member countries unanimously agreed to release 400 million barrels of emergency oil. The largest release in the agency's 52-year history. More than double the Ukraine 2022 release. More than six times the Katrina 2005 release. "The oil market challenges we are facing are unprecedented in scale." — Birol, Paris, March 11. The analyst response, within minutes: Macquarie research firm: the 400 million barrels covers roughly 4 days of global demand and 16 days of Hormuz transit volume. One investment bank: "Crude oil will continue to trade like a meme stock until the solution is peace." This is the economic logic nobody is explaining for non-specialists: The IEA reserve exists to bridge supply gaps during emergencies. It is not designed to replace a major global trade route indefinitely. It is a bandage. The wound is the Strait of Hormuz. Hormuz export volumes are currently at less than 10% of pre-war levels. The emergency stockpile buys 4 days. The war is on Day 12. There is no reserve large enough to replace a closed Strait for weeks or months. "The solution is peace." Peace has a price. Nobody has announced what America is willing to pay for it.

💬 Debate ThisThe world's largest emergency oil release covers 4 days. The war is on Day 12. At what point does "emergency reserves" become evidence that the world has no plan — and who is accountable when they run out?
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: There’s widespread doubt that the release of emergency stockpiles can be swift enough to plug the yawning supply gap, writes @oil_gs01 https://t.co/0PAGYGg84e

The IEA just fired its biggest weapon. And the market barely flinched. This morning, IEA Director Fatih Birol announced all 32 member countries unanimously agreed to release 400 million barrels of emergency oil. The largest release in the agency's 52-year history. More than double the Ukraine 2022 release. More than six times the Katrina 2005 release. "The oil market challenges we are facing are unprecedented in scale." — Birol, Paris, March 11. The analyst response, within minutes: Macquarie research firm: the 400 million barrels covers roughly 4 days of global demand and 16 days of Hormuz transit volume. One investment bank: "Crude oil will continue to trade like a meme stock until the solution is peace." This is the economic logic nobody is explaining for non-specialists: The IEA reserve exists to bridge supply gaps during emergencies. It is not designed to replace a major global trade route indefinitely. It is a bandage. The wound is the Strait of Hormuz. Hormuz export volumes are currently at less than 10% of pre-war levels. The emergency stockpile buys 4 days. The war is on Day 12. There is no reserve large enough to replace a closed Strait for weeks or months. "The solution is peace." Peace has a price. Nobody has announced what America is willing to pay for it.

💬 Debate ThisThe world's largest emergency oil release covers 4 days. The war is on Day 12. At what point does "emergency reserves" become evidence that the world has no plan — and who is accountable when they run out?

Dự báo giá Dầu Thô của một số quốc gia, tổ chức, ngân hàng, chuyên gia hàng đầu:

1. 🇮🇷Iran: Cảnh báo thế giới phải chuẩn bị cho giá dầu 200 USD/thùng nếu Trump tiếp tục làm tình hình thêm căng thẳng.

2. Ngân hàng Liên bang 🇦🇺Úc (CBA) cảnh báo rằng cuộc khủng hoảng Iran có thể khiến giá dầu thô Brent tăng lên 150 đô la Mỹ.

3. Chuyên gia người 🇷🇺Nga Iqbal Guliyev dự đoán giá dầu sẽ đạt 150-160/thùng nếu chiến tranh Trung Đông kéo dài.

4. 🇶🇦Qatar cảnh báo các nước xuất khẩu vùng Vịnh có thể đồng loạt ngừng sản xuất, giá dầu có thể tăng lên 150 USD/thùng  

5. Macquarie Research: Nếu eo Hormuz đóng cửa trong vài tuần, giá dầu có thể vọt lên 150 USD/thùng hoặc cao hơn.

6. Wood Mackenzie / Rystad Energy (theo các phân tích gần đây): 2 tháng gián đoạn → 110+ USD; 4 tháng → 135 USD; trường hợp cực đoan (kéo dài + thiệt hại lớn) có thể chạm 200 USD. 

7. WSJ và các analyst khác: Giá dầu điều chỉnh lạm phát có thể đạt 215 USD/thùng nếu Hormuz bị chặn hoàn toàn lâu dài. 

Hầu hết đều đồng ý: giá dầu sẽ tăng mạnh (ít nhất 100 USD/thùng trong vài tuần, có thể 150-200+ USD nếu kéo dài 1-2 tháng hoặc hạ tầng bị tấn công). Điều này sẽ gây lạm phát toàn cầu, đẩy giá xăng dầu, hàng hóa và có nguy cơ suy thoái (giống 1970s).

tweets.text:
Breaking.
The IEA just announced the largest emergency oil reserve release in its 52-year history.  400 million barrels.
Approved unanimously by all 32 member countries.  IEA Director Fatih Birol, in Paris, just said: "The oil market challenges we are facing are unprecedented in scale."  Compare that to previous releases:
→ 2022, Russia invades Ukraine: 182 million barrels released
→ 2011, Libya war: 60 million barrels
→ 2005, Hurricane Katrina: 60 million barrels
→ 2026, Iran war: 400 million barrels  More than double any previous action. Ever.
And here is the analyst verdict, published within minutes of the announcement.
Macquarie research firm, cited by Reuters:  The 400 million barrels covers roughly 4 days of normal global demand and 16 days of the volume that used to transit through the Strait of Hormuz.  "If that doesn't sound like much, it isn't."  One investment bank put it plainly: "Crude oil will continue to trade like a meme stock until the solution is peace."  Hormuz export volumes are currently at LESS than 10% of pre-war levels.
The emergency stockpile is the largest in history.  It is also, by the IEA's own admission, not a permanent solution.  The Strait is still closed.
The ships are still burning.
The price of peace is the only number that matters.  Nobody has set it yet.

🏷️ @IEA_org @CNBC

tweets.text:
📉 LA MONTAÑA RUSA DE ORMUZ: DEL PÁNICO AL ALIVIO 🛢️⚡

El mercado ha vivido hoy un "lunes negro" que terminó en verde gracias, en gran parte, a la narrativa de victoria del presidente.

📍 La Volatilidad del Día

🚀 El Pico del Amanecer: Debido al cierre de facto del Estrecho de Ormuz y los ataques a refinerías iraníes, el Brent llegó a rozar los 120 dólares durante la madrugada.

📉 El Desplome de la Tarde: Tras las palabras de Trump asegurando que la guerra está "prácticamente completa" y que Irán "no tiene armada ni fuerza aérea", el precio cayó en picado, cerrando en torno a los 85-90 dólares.

⛽ Gasolina en EE. UU.: Aunque el crudo bajó hoy, el precio medio en los surtidores estadounidenses tocó los 3,45 dólares por galón ayer domingo, un aumento del 16% en una semana.

💡 Análisis: ¿Por qué Trump lo llama "solo una incursión"?

Para el presidente, el costo económico actual es un "pequeño precio" comparado con el beneficio estratégico:

"Tenía que hacerse": Trump insiste en que eliminar la amenaza nuclear de Irán (Operación Martillo de Medianoche) es la única forma de garantizar precios bajos a largo plazo. Su argumento es: "Prefiero un pico de dos semanas ahora que un chantaje nuclear para siempre".

"Nos estamos acercando a terminar": Al decir que la operación está llegando a su fin, Trump busca calmar a los inversores. Si los mercados creen que el conflicto durará "semanas y no meses" (como dijo hoy el Secretario de Energía Chris Wright), el precio del petróleo dejará de incluir la "prima de guerra".

La Estrategia de la Reserva: Parte de la bajada de hoy se debió a que el G7 y la IEA discutieron la liberación de reservas estratégicas, aunque Trump ha insinuado que su "plan" personal (del cual dijo que estaríamos "muy felices") es lo que realmente ha estabilizado el mercado.

📉 El Factor de los "Fools" (Necios)
Trump ha sido tajante en Truth Social hoy: "Solo los necios" pensarían que el aumento temporal de precios es peor que un Irán nuclear. Esta retórica busca blindar a su base ante las críticas de los demócratas, quienes hoy (liderados por Chuck Schumer) han calificado la subida de precios como la "Guerra de Elección de Trump".

Dato Clave: A pesar del optimismo de Trump, analistas de Macquarie Research advierten que si el Estrecho de Ormuz permanece bloqueado por restos de naufragios o minas solo dos semanas más, el petróleo podría rebotar por encima de los 150 dólares, invalidando el discurso de "incursión corta".

tweets.text:
The CLARITY Act isn't enabling new products.

It's releasing products that already exist.

Bernstein Research estimates more than 40 institutional tokenized fund products are currently in SEC preregistration or registration review, representing a $15 to $25 billion pipeline of new on-chain RWA assets. 

These aren't ideas. They're products that completed internal approval, cleared legal review, passed compliance sign off, and are sitting in holding patterns waiting for regulatory classification to settle.

The CLARITY Act is a gate, not a starting line.

When it passes, 40+ products don't launch gradually. They launch into a market that already has $33B in distributed assets, 792,000 holders, and infrastructure that didn't exist 18 months ago.

The current $33B figure represents what launched without regulatory certainty.

The $15 - 25B pipeline represents what was built specifically for the moment that certainty arrived.

That moment is Q3 2026.

The queue is longer than the current market suggests.
tweets.full_text:
The CLARITY Act isn't enabling new products.

It's releasing products that already exist.

Bernstein Research estimates more than 40 institutional tokenized fund products are currently in SEC preregistration or registration review, representing a $15 to $25 billion pipeline of new on-chain RWA assets. 

These aren't ideas. They're products that completed internal approval, cleared legal review, passed compliance sign off, and are sitting in holding patterns waiting for regulatory classification to settle.

The CLARITY Act is a gate, not a starting line.

When it passes, 40+ products don't launch gradually. They launch into a market that already has $33B in distributed assets, 792,000 holders, and infrastructure that didn't exist 18 months ago.

The current $33B figure represents what launched without regulatory certainty.

The $15 - 25B pipeline represents what was built specifically for the moment that certainty arrived.

That moment is Q3 2026.

The queue is longer than the current market suggests.

tweets.text:
The asset management industry has a blind spot.

Bernstein Research, a premier global research and brokerage firm, just published one of the most comprehensive surveys of AI adoption across asset management, based on 20 vertical AI vendors and symposium data.

Their thesis is that 2026 is the last window to embrace AI. Laggards who adopt too slowly will become structurally disadvantaged.

Bernstein’s headline findings are striking:

→ 46% of fund managers now use Gen AI for investment research, up from 35% in 2023

→ 90%+ of firms expect to have 6 or more use cases in production within two years

→ The shift is from "productivity" to "alpha generation"

→ Top 50 global asset managers have grown AUM at 7% CAGR but revenue at just 4% over the last decade

In other words, AI is being framed as a survival mechanism in a world where AUM growth no longer translates to revenue growth.

But what is missing from their 80+ page report = Capital formation.

Every use case Bernstein catalogues is about deploying capital: research synthesis, portfolio construction, risk monitoring, trade execution. Every vertical AI tool that was surveyed in the report (e.g., AlphaSense, Rogo, Hebbia, LinqAlpha, https://t.co/PS7n9E1Bhl, Finster) is focused on the investment workflow. The PM is the key user.

Nothing in the study focuses on the fund managers and marketers looking to attract investors / allocators. 

Yet look at the actual fundraising environment private markets GPs are operating in:

→ Global PE fundraising fell 12.7% in 2025 to $480B (the 3rd consecutive year of decline)

→ VC fundraising dropped below $100B for the first time since 2015

→ The average VC fund now takes 17.5 months to close. This is longer than ever

→ DPI as a share of PE AUM hit 6% in mid-2025. The 2015-2019 average was 16%

→ 82% of emerging hedge fund managers cite raising capital as their single biggest challenge

DM me if you would like a copy of this research.

While the investment side of asset management is getting increasingly AI-native, the capital formation side is still running on Outlook, spreadsheets, and CRM systems from 2000-2010. Clearly, not using AI for fundraising will also become a structural advantage.

So if 2026 is the year that AI becomes table stakes for investment teams, 2027 is the year it becomes table stakes for capital formation teams. The GPs who figure this out first will raise faster and drop fewer LPs.

This is what we are building at TOMO. A Capital Formation OS for hedge fund, PE, and VC GPs. Our Founding Circle is onboarding in June/July 2026. We are working with a small cohort of fund managers and marketers who want to shape how this category gets built.

If you are a GP thinking about what AI-native fundraising actually looks like, let’s talk.  

Link here:
https://t.co/JMusdBTWom

tweets.text:
中國半導體自給率大躍進　2026年目標本土矽晶圓使用率達七成

【華夏新聞社北京/台北報導】中國大力推動半導體供應鏈本土化，據日經亞洲最新獨家報導，中國設定2026年底前，國內晶片製造商使用的矽晶圓超過七成須來自本土供應商，此舉標誌著北京在AI時代加速關鍵材料自給自足的重大步伐。

報導指出，以西安奕斯偉材料科技（Xi’an Eswin）為首的本土企業正積極擴張產能。奕斯偉目標2026年達到每月120萬片12吋矽晶圓產能，可滿足中國國內約40%需求，其全球市佔率預計超過10%。該公司目前在西安和武漢新建廠房，今年將新增每月70萬片產能。

根據Bernstein Research分析，中國矽晶圓全球產能份額已從2020年的3%躍升至2025年的28%，預計2026年進一步增至32%。2025年中國已能自給約50%的12吋晶圓需求，2026年這一比例將繼續上升。其他主要本土廠商包括國家矽業集團、中環先進半導體及杭州獅邁微電子等，亦同步擴大規模。

此一本土化攻勢正值中美科技競爭白熱化之際。面對出口管制與全球供應鏈重組，中國藉由巨額補貼與國家戰略，加速填補高端材料缺口。分析認為，這不僅提升晶片製造韌性，更將重塑全球半導體產業格局。

業界人士指出，矽晶圓是晶片製造基礎材料，七成自給目標達成後，中國在先進製程的自主能力將大幅躍升，為AI、電動車及5G等領域提供堅實支撐。
tweets.author_name: 華夏新聞社 Huasia News Agency
tweets.full_text:
中國半導體自給率大躍進　2026年目標本土矽晶圓使用率達七成

【華夏新聞社北京/台北報導】中國大力推動半導體供應鏈本土化，據日經亞洲最新獨家報導，中國設定2026年底前，國內晶片製造商使用的矽晶圓超過七成須來自本土供應商，此舉標誌著北京在AI時代加速關鍵材料自給自足的重大步伐。

報導指出，以西安奕斯偉材料科技（Xi’an Eswin）為首的本土企業正積極擴張產能。奕斯偉目標2026年達到每月120萬片12吋矽晶圓產能，可滿足中國國內約40%需求，其全球市佔率預計超過10%。該公司目前在西安和武漢新建廠房，今年將新增每月70萬片產能。

根據Bernstein Research分析，中國矽晶圓全球產能份額已從2020年的3%躍升至2025年的28%，預計2026年進一步增至32%。2025年中國已能自給約50%的12吋晶圓需求，2026年這一比例將繼續上升。其他主要本土廠商包括國家矽業集團、中環先進半導體及杭州獅邁微電子等，亦同步擴大規模。

此一本土化攻勢正值中美科技競爭白熱化之際。面對出口管制與全球供應鏈重組，中國藉由巨額補貼與國家戰略，加速填補高端材料缺口。分析認為，這不僅提升晶片製造韌性，更將重塑全球半導體產業格局。

業界人士指出，矽晶圓是晶片製造基礎材料，七成自給目標達成後，中國在先進製程的自主能力將大幅躍升，為AI、電動車及5G等領域提供堅實支撐。

tweets.text:
Nikkei Asia

Çin 2026 sonuna kadar gelişmiş silikon wafer kullanımında yüzde 70 yerli üretim hedefliyor

🔹 Çin hükümeti çip üretiminde kullanılan gelişmiş silikon wafer’ların yüzde 70’inin yerli üretimden karşılanmasını hedefliyor.

🔹 Hedef özellikle 12 inçlik gelişmiş silikon wafer kullanımında dış bağımlılığı azaltmayı amaçlıyor.

🔹 Kaynaklar bu hedefin Çinli çip üreticileri için “örtülü zorunluluk” haline geldiğini söyledi.

🔹 Çin’in diğer teknoloji kendine yeterlilik hedeflerinde geride kalmasına rağmen silikon wafer hedefinin büyük ölçüde gerçekleşmesinin beklendiği belirtildi.

🔹 Çinli yöneticiler yerli wafer’ların özellikle olgun ve eski nesil çip üretiminde artık yeterli kaliteye ulaştığını ifade etti.

🔹 Daha gelişmiş çip üretiminde ise yabancı lider şirketlerin desteğine hâlen ihtiyaç duyulduğu belirtildi.

🔹 Silikon wafer’lar mantık ve bellek çiplerinin üretildiği temel yarı iletken yüzeyi olarak kullanılıyor.

🔹 12 inçlik wafer’lar modern mantık ve bellek çiplerinde kullanılırken 8 inçlik wafer’lar daha eski nesil çiplerde tercih ediliyor.

🔹 Çin’in 8 inç wafer üretiminde büyük ölçüde kendine yeterli hale geldiği belirtildi.

🔹 Xi’an Eswin Material Technology Çin’in en büyük silikon wafer şirketlerinden biri olarak öne çıkıyor.

🔹 Şirket geçen yıl Şanghay STAR borsasında halka arz edildi.

🔹 Eswin 2026 itibarıyla aylık toplam 1,2 milyon wafer üretim kapasitesine ulaşmayı hedefliyor.

🔹 Şirket bu kapasiteyle Çin’in 12 inç wafer talebinin yaklaşık yüzde 40’ını karşılamayı planlıyor.

🔹 Eswin’in küresel pazar payının yüzde 10’u aşmasının beklendiği belirtildi.

🔹 National Silicon Industry Group, Zhonghuan Advanced ve Hangzhou Lion Microelectronics diğer önemli Çinli wafer üreticileri arasında gösterildi.

🔹 Bu şirketlerin de üretim kapasitesini genişlettiği ifade edildi.

🔹 Eswin Xi’an ve Wuhan şehirlerinde yeni üretim tesisleri kuruyor.

🔹 Şirket bu yıl aylık 700 bin wafer ek kapasite oluşturmayı planlıyor.

🔹 Kaynaklar Eswin’in Çin’deki toplam kapasite genişlemesinin neredeyse yarısını tek başına gerçekleştirebileceğini söyledi.

🔹 Eswin’in Micron, TSMC, GlobalFoundries ve UMC dahil birçok küresel şirkete tedarik sağladığı belirtildi.

🔹 Samsung Electronics ve SK Hynix’in de Eswin ürünlerini doğrulama sürecinde olduğu açıklandı.

🔹 Eswin’in 2025 geliri 2,64 milyar yuana yükseldi.

🔹 Şirketin henüz kâra geçemediği belirtildi.

🔹 SMIC, Hua Hong Semiconductor, CXMT ve YMTC Eswin’in büyük müşterileri arasında yer alıyor.

🔹 Eswin yerli wafer’ların yeni Çin çip fabrikalarında “varsayılan seçenek” haline geldiğini açıkladı.

🔹 SMIC ve Huawei bağlantılı şirketlerin 7 nanometre ve 5 nanometre seviyesine yakın gelişmiş çip kapasitesini hızla artırdığı belirtildi.

🔹 Bu genişlemenin Çin’in yapay zekâ işlem gücü ihtiyacını karşılamayı hedeflediği ifade edildi.

🔹 Bernstein Research analisti David Dai, Çin’in 2025 itibarıyla 12 inç wafer talebinin yaklaşık yüzde 50’sini yerli üretimle karşılayabildiğini tahmin etti.

🔹 Yerli üretim oranının 2026’da daha da yükselmesinin beklendiği belirtildi.

🔹 Çinli üreticilerin küresel wafer kapasitesindeki payı 2020’de yüzde 3 seviyesindeydi.

🔹 Bu oran 2025’te yaklaşık yüzde 28’e yükseldi.

🔹 Küresel kapasite payının 2026’da yüzde 32’ye çıkmasının beklendiği açıklandı.

🔹 Üretim kalitesi ve kapasite kullanım oranlarının gerçek üretim seviyesini belirleyeceği ifade edildi.

🔹 Küresel silikon wafer pazarı uzun yıllar Japon ve Tayvanlı şirketlerin hakimiyetinde kaldı.

🔹 Shinetsu Chemical, Sumco ve GlobalWafers sektörün geleneksel liderleri arasında gösterildi.

🔹 Çin’in başlangıçta yalnızca iç pazara odaklandığı ancak zamanla küresel rolünün büyüyebileceği belirtildi.

🔹 Çinli şirketlerin pazara hızlı girişi nedeniyle küresel arz fazlası endişeleri oluştuğu ifade edildi.

🔹 Buna rağmen yapay zekâ altyapı yatırımları nedeniyle silikon wafer talebinin arttığı belirtildi.

🔹 Gelişmiş çip paketleme teknolojilerinin birden fazla wafer kullanımını artırdığı ifade edildi.

🔹 SEMI verilerine göre küresel silikon wafer sevkiyatlarının 2026’da yüzde 13 artması bekleniyor.

🔹 SMIC’in tasarım müşterilerinden yerli wafer kullanımını kabul etmelerini istediği belirtildi.

🔹 Amaç yerli wafer’ların farklı çip uygulamalarında doğrulanmasını hızlandırmak olarak açıklandı.

🔹 Çinli ekran üreticisi BOE Technology de sürücü çipi tedarikçilerinden yerli wafer kullanımını talep etti.

🔹 Driver IC olarak bilinen sürücü çipleri ekran ve motor sistemlerinde kullanılıyor.

🔹 Çinli şirketlerin pazara hızlı girişi nedeniyle küresel arz fazlası endişeleri oluştuğu ifade edildi.

🔹 Buna rağmen yapay zekâ altyapı yatırımları nedeniyle silikon wafer talebinin arttığı belirtildi.

🔹 Gelişmiş çip paketleme teknolojilerinin birden fazla wafer kullanımını artırdığı ifade edildi.

🔹 SEMI verilerine göre küresel silikon wafer sevkiyatlarının 2026’da yüzde 13 artması bekleniyor.

🔹 SMIC’in tasarım müşterilerinden yerli wafer kullanımını kabul etmelerini istediği belirtildi.

🔹 Amaç yerli wafer’ların farklı çip uygulamalarında doğrulanmasını hızlandırmak olarak açıklandı.

🔹 Çinli ekran üreticisi BOE Technology de sürücü çipi tedarikçilerinden yerli wafer kullanımını talep etti.

🔹 Driver IC olarak bilinen sürücü çipleri ekran ve motor sistemlerinde kullanılıyor.

tweets.text:
Tech界隈はLLMのベンチマーク競争で盛り上がっているが、Bernstein Researchは「AIエコノミーの本当のボトルネックは物理インフラにある」と指摘しています。

電力・冷却・データセンター建設コストが律速要因——モデルがいくら賢くなっても、運ぶインフラが足りなければ恩恵は届きません。

インフラ投資の規模と対象企業の動向を今のうちに確認するのが先決です。
tweets.author_name: AIらぼ🎙️暗号資産・エアドロ大学
