Tech界隈はLLMのベンチマーク競争で盛り上がっているが、Bernstein Researchは「AIエコノミーの本当のボトルネックは物理インフラにある」と指摘しています。

電力・冷却・データセンター建設コストが律速要因——モデルがいくら賢くなっても、運ぶインフラが足りなければ恩恵は届きません。

インフラ投資の規模と対象企業の動向を今のうちに確認するのが先決です。

tweets.text:
AMD 2026년 1분기 어닝콜 Q&A 세션  $AMD

질문 1: 서버 CPU 시장 규모(TAM)와 시장 점유율
질문 (TD Cowen, 조슈아 북할터): 애널리스트 데이에서 발표한 600억 달러 규모의 2030년 서버 CPU 시장 전망치가 왜 이렇게 빨리 두 배(1,200억 달러)로 늘어났습니까? 또한 경쟁사(인텔 및 ARM 기반 업체)들의 공세 속에서 점유율 50% 이상 달성 목표에 대해 얼마나 확신하시나요?

답변 (리사 수): 에이전틱 AI와 추론 수요가 우리가 예상했던 것보다 훨씬 빠르게 CPU 수요를 끌어올리고 있습니다. AI 채택이 확대됨에 따라 더 많은 추론 작업이 필요하며, 이는 곧 데이터 처리와 오케스트레이션을 위한 더 많은 CPU 연산을 의미합니다. AMD는 단순한 하나의 칩이 아니라 성능, 전력, 비용, AI 인프라에 최적화된 '베니스(Venice)' 제품군과 같은 광범위한 포트폴리오를 구축하고 있습니다. 현재 고객들의 반응과 로드맵을 볼 때, 50% 이상의 시장 점유율 달성을 매우 낙관하고 있습니다.

질문 2: Instinct(MI450/Helios) 수요 및 고객층
질문 (TD Cowen, 조슈아 북할터): MI450과 헬리오스(Helios)에 대한 고객 예측치가 초기 계획을 상회한다고 하셨는데, 이것이 기존 파트너(오픈AI, 메타)의 주문 증량 때문인가요, 아니면 새로운 고객들의 유입 때문인가요?

답변 (리사 수): 두 가지 모두입니다. 대규모 파트너들과의 공동 엔지니어링은 매우 순조롭습니다. 동시에 MI450 시리즈를 대규모로 도입하려는 고객층이 매우 넓어지고 있습니다. 훈련뿐만 아니라 특히 대규모 추론 배포를 원하는 새로운 고객들이 늘고 있으며, 이 덕분에 2027년까지 연평균 성장률(CAGR) 80% 이상을 달성할 수 있다는 경로가 명확해졌습니다.

질문 3: 데이터 센터 매출 추이와 공급망
질문 (Barclays, 토마스 오말리): 1분기 서버 CPU는 50% 이상 성장했는데 GPU는 상대적으로 주춤한 것 같습니다. 올해 하반기 및 내년 공급망(전력, 데이터 센터 구축 등) 이슈에 대해 어떻게 대비하고 계신가요?

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 1분기 데이터 센터 AI 매출이 소폭 감소한 것은 중국 시장 관련 매출 전환기 때문입니다. 하지만 2분기에는 서버 CPU와 GPU 모두 두 자릿수 성장을 예상합니다. 특히 서버 CPU는 2분기에 전년 대비 70% 이상 성장��� 것입니다. 헬리오스(MI450 기반)는 3분기에 초기 물량을 시작으로 4분기에 본격적인 양산에 들어갑니다. 공급망은 여전히 타이트하지만, 우리는 2027년 성장에 필요한 웨이퍼 및 후공정 용량을 확보하고 있으며 전력 수급 문제도 고객사들과 밀접하게 논의 중입니다.

질문 4: CPU 차별화 및 경쟁 구도
질문 (Deutsche Bank, 로스 세이모어): 경쟁사들이 자체 ARM CPU를 개발하거나 x86 경쟁사의 공급이 원활해지는 상황에서 AMD만의 차별점은 무엇입니까?

답변 (리사 수): 핵심은 '광범위한 포트폴리오'입니다. 하이퍼스케일러들은 특정 용도에 맞는 다양한 CPU를 필요로 합니다. 단순히 ARM이냐 x86이냐의 문제가 아니라, 워크로드별(헤드 노드, 에이전틱 AI 전용 등)로 최적화된 칩을 누가 제공하느냐가 중요합니다. AMD는 베니스 세대에서 '베라노(Verano)'와 같은 AI 최적화 CPU를 추가하며 가장 강력한 라인업을 갖췄습니다.

질문 5: 매출 총이익률(Gross Margin) 전망
질문 (Deutsche Bank, 로스 세이모어): 하반기에 MI450이 대거 출하되면 이익률이 다소 희석될 수 있는데, 이를 상쇄할 수 있는 요인은 무엇입니까?

답변 (진 후): 몇 가지 강력한 순풍이 있습니다. 첫째, 수익성이 높은 서버 CPU가 2분기 70% 이상 성장하며 이익을 견인합니다. 둘째, 수익성이 낮은 게이밍 매출 비중이 줄어듭니다. 셋째, 임베디드 부문의 성장이 이익률에 기여합니다. MI450이 전사 평균보다 이익률이 낮아 4분기에 일부 영향은 있겠지만, 전반적인 제품 믹스 개선을 통해 장기 목표치인 55~58%를 향해 순항 중입니다.

질문 6: 서버 CPU의 성장 동력(판매량 vs 가격)
질문 (UBS, 티모시 아큐리): 서버 CPU 성장이 판매량 증가 때문인가요, 아니면 가격(ASP) 상승 때문인가요? 하반기에도 가격 인상이 예정되어 있습니까?

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 1분기 성장은 주로 '판매량(Unit)'이 주도했습니다. 제노아(Genoa)와 투린(Turin) 모두 많이 팔렸습니다. 물론 코어 수가 늘어남에 따라 자연스러운 평균 판매 단가(ASP) 상승 효과가 있습니다. 공급망 비용 상승으로 인한 일부 가격 조정은 있지만, 우리의 전략은 가격 인상보다는 시장 점유율 확대와 판매량 극대화에 초점을 맞추고 있습니다.

질문 7: 에이전틱 AI CPU 수요의 성격
질문 (BofA, 비벡 아리야): 에이전틱 AI용 CPU 수요가 늘어나는 것이 GPU 수요를 대체하는 것입니까, 아니면 별도의 추가 수요입니까?

답변 (리사 수): 완전히 '추가적인(Incremental)' 수요입니다. 기본 모델을 구동하기 위해 가속기(GPU)가 필요하다면, 그 위에서 구동되는 AI 에이전트들이 복잡한 작업을 수행하면서 더 많은 CPU 연산을 유발합니다. 과거에는 CPU와 GPU 비율이 1:8 정도였다면, 이제는 1:1에 가까워지고 있습니다. 즉, GPU가 많이 팔릴수록 CPU도 함께 더 많이 필요한 구조로 변했습니다.

질문 8: 메모리 가격 상승 영향
질문 (BofA, 비벡 아리야): 고대역폭 메모리(HBM) 등 메모리 가격 상승이 비용 부담이 될 텐데, 공급 물량은 충분히 확보하셨나요?

답변 (리사 수): 메모리 업체들과 매우 깊은 파트너십을 맺고 있으며, 우리가 설정한 목표치를 달성하고도 남을 만큼 충분한 물량을 확보했습니다. 다만 메모리 가격 상승이 PC나 게이밍 같은 소비자 시장의 수요에는 다소 부정적인 영향을 줄 수 있어, 하반기 가이던스에 이를 보수적으로 반영했습니다.

질문 9: PC 시장 및 수익성 전략
질문 (Cantor Fitzgerald, C.J. 뮤즈): 하반기 PC 시장의 계절성과 수익성에 대해 어떻게 보시나요?

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 상업용 PC와 프리미엄 AI PC 시장은 매우 강력합니다. 다만 저가형 데스크톱 시장은 메모리 비용 상승으로 다소 위축될 수 있습니다. 우리는 프리미엄 제품군에 집중하여 시장 평균보다 높은 성장을 유지할 것입니다. 인스팅트(GPU) 사업은 현재 매출 성장에 우선순위를 두고 있지만, 규모의 경제가 실현됨에 따라 향후 비용 절감을 통해 수익성을 개선할 여지가 큽니다.

질문 10: 연구개발(R&D) 및 운영 비용(OpEx)
질문 (Bernstein Research, 스테이시 라스곤 / Jefferies, 블레인 커티스): 운영 비용이 가이드라인을 계속 상회하는 이유는 무엇입니까? 판매관리비(SG&A)보다 R&D 투자가 더 중요해 보이는데 향후 비중은 어떻게 됩니까?

답변 (진 후/리사 수): 매출 성장이 예상보다 강력했기 때문에 그에 따른 변동 비용이 발생했습니다. 하지만 가장 중요한 이유는 AI 기회를 선점하기 위해 R&D에 공격적으로 투자하고 있기 때문입니다. 향후 R&D 비용은 SG&A보다 훨씬 빠르게 증가할 것입니다. SG&A 투자는 우리가 과거에 취약했던 기업용 서버와 상업용 PC 시장의 영업력을 강화하는 데 쓰이고 있으며, 이는 실제 매출 성장으로 증명되고 있습니다.

질문 11: 구형 제품 생산 및 공급 전략
질문 (Jefferies, 블레인 커티스): 경쟁사들은 구형 공정(7나노 등)을 다시 가동한다는 이야기가 있는데, AMD도 구형 제품을 더 오래 판매할 계획이 있습니까?

답변 (리사 수): 우리는 고객들이 항상 최신 제품을 원한다는 것을 알고 있습니다. 투린(Turin)은 이번 분기 매출의 50%를 넘겼고, 제노아(Genoa)도 매우 강력합니다. 최신 제품이 전력 효율과 성능 면에서 압도적이기 때문입니다. 우리는 구형 제품에 매달리기보다 베니스(Venice)와 같은 차세대 제품으로 빠르게 전환하여 시장을 리드하는 데 집중하고 있습니다. 2027~2028년 수요를 미리 논의하며 공급망을 장기적으로 설계하고 있습니다.

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 1분기 데이터 센터 AI 매출이 소폭 감소한 것은 중국 시장 관련 매출 전환기 때문입니다. 하지만 2분기에는 서버 CPU와 GPU 모두 두 자릿수 성장을 예상합니다. 특히 서버 CPU는 2분기에 전년 대비 70% 이상 성장��� 것입니다. 헬리오스(MI450 기반)는 3분기에 초기 물량을 시작으로 4분기에 본격적인 양산에 들어갑니다. 공급망은 여전히 타이트하지만, 우리는 2027년 성장에 필요한 웨이퍼 및 후공정 용량을 확보하고 있으며 전력 수급 문제도 고객사들과 밀접하게 논의 중입니다.

질문 4: CPU 차별화 및 경쟁 구도
질문 (Deutsche Bank, 로스 세이모어): 경쟁사들이 자체 ARM CPU를 개발하거나 x86 경쟁사의 공급이 원활해지는 상황에서 AMD만의 차별점은 무엇입니까?

답변 (리사 수): 핵심은 '광범위한 포트폴리오'입니다. 하이퍼스케일러들은 특정 용도에 맞는 다양한 CPU를 필요로 합니다. 단순히 ARM이냐 x86이냐의 문제가 아니라, 워크로드별(헤드 노드, 에이전틱 AI 전용 등)로 최적화된 칩을 누가 제공하느냐가 중요합니다. AMD는 베니스 세대에서 '베라노(Verano)'와 같은 AI 최적화 CPU를 추가하며 가장 강력한 라인업을 갖췄습니다.

질문 5: 매출 총이익률(Gross Margin) 전망
질문 (Deutsche Bank, 로스 세이모어): 하반기에 MI450이 대거 출하되면 이익률이 다소 희석될 수 있는데, 이를 상쇄할 수 있는 요인은 무엇입니까?

답변 (진 후): 몇 가지 강력한 순풍이 있습니다. 첫째, 수익성이 높은 서버 CPU가 2분기 70% 이상 성장하며 이익을 견인합니다. 둘째, 수익성이 낮은 게이밍 매출 비중이 줄어듭니다. 셋째, 임베디드 부문의 성장이 이익률에 기여합니다. MI450이 전사 평균보다 이익률이 낮아 4분기에 일부 영향은 있겠지만, 전반적인 제품 믹스 개선을 통해 장기 목표치인 55~58%를 향해 순항 중입니다.

질문 6: 서버 CPU의 성장 동력(판매량 vs 가격)
질문 (UBS, 티모시 아큐리): 서버 CPU 성장이 판매량 증가 때문인가요, 아니면 가격(ASP) 상승 때문인가요? 하반기에도 가격 인상이 예정되어 있습니까?

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 1분기 성장은 주로 '판매량(Unit)'이 주도했습니다. 제노아(Genoa)와 투린(Turin) 모두 많이 팔렸습니다. 물론 코어 수가 늘어남에 따라 자연스러운 평균 판매 단가(ASP) 상승 효과가 있습니다. 공급망 비용 상승으로 인한 일부 가격 조정은 있지만, 우리의 전략은 가격 인상보다는 시장 점유율 확대와 판매량 극대화에 초점을 맞추고 있습니다.

질문 7: 에이전틱 AI CPU 수요의 성격
질문 (BofA, 비벡 아리야): 에이전틱 AI용 CPU 수요가 늘어나는 것이 GPU 수요를 대체하는 것입니까, 아니면 별도의 추가 수요입니까?

답변 (리사 수): 완전히 '추가적인(Incremental)' 수요입니다. 기본 모델을 구동하기 위해 가속기(GPU)가 필요하다면, 그 위에서 구동되는 AI 에이전트들이 복잡한 작업을 수행하면서 더 많은 CPU 연산을 유발합니다. 과거에는 CPU와 GPU 비율이 1:8 정도였다면, 이제는 1:1에 가까워지고 있습니다. 즉, GPU가 많이 팔릴수록 CPU도 함께 더 많이 필요한 구조로 변했습니다.

질문 8: 메모리 가격 상승 영향
질문 (BofA, 비벡 아리야): 고대역폭 메모리(HBM) 등 메모리 가격 상승이 비용 부담이 될 텐데, 공급 물량은 충분히 확보하셨나요?

답변 (리사 수): 메모리 업체들과 매우 깊은 파트너십을 맺고 있으며, 우리가 설정한 목표치를 달성하고도 남을 만큼 충분한 물량을 확보했습니다. 다만 메모리 가격 상승이 PC나 게이밍 같은 소비자 시장의 수요에는 다소 부정적인 영향을 줄 수 있어, 하반기 가이던스에 이를 보수적으로 반영했습니다.

질문 9: PC 시장 및 수익성 전략
질문 (Cantor Fitzgerald, C.J. 뮤즈): 하반기 PC 시장의 계절성과 수익성에 대해 어떻게 보시나요?

답변 (리사 수/진 후): 상업용 PC와 프리미엄 AI PC 시장은 매우 강력합니다. 다만 저가형 데스크톱 시장은 메모리 비용 상승으로 다소 위축될 수 있습니다. 우리는 프리미엄 제품군에 집중하여 시장 평균보다 높은 성장을 유지할 것입니다. 인스팅트(GPU) 사업은 현재 매출 성장에 우선순위를 두고 있지만, 규모의 경제가 실현됨에 따라 향후 비용 절감을 통해 수익성을 개선할 여지가 큽니다.

질문 10: 연구개발(R&D) 및 운영 비용(OpEx)
질문 (Bernstein Research, 스테이시 라스곤 / Jefferies, 블레인 커티스): 운영 비용이 가이드라인을 계속 상회하는 이유는 무엇입니까? 판매관리비(SG&A)보다 R&D 투자가 더 중요해 보이는데 향후 비중은 어떻게 됩니까?

답변 (진 후/리사 수): 매출 성장이 예상보다 강력했기 때문에 그에 따른 변동 비용이 발생했습니다. 하지만 가장 중요한 이유는 AI 기회를 선점하기 위해 R&D에 공격적으로 투자하고 있기 때문입니다. 향후 R&D 비용은 SG&A보다 훨씬 빠르게 증가할 것입니다. SG&A 투자는 우리가 과거에 취약했던 기업용 서버와 상업용 PC 시장의 영업력을 강화하는 데 쓰이고 있으며, 이는 실제 매출 성장으로 증명되고 있습니다.

질문 11: 구형 제품 생산 및 공급 전략
질문 (Jefferies, 블레인 커티스): 경쟁사들은 구형 공정(7나노 등)을 다시 가동한다는 이야기가 있는데, AMD도 구형 제품을 더 오래 판매할 계획이 있습니까?

답변 (리사 수): 우리는 고객들이 항상 최신 제품을 원한다는 것을 알고 있습니다. 투린(Turin)은 이번 분기 매출의 50%를 넘겼고, 제노아(Genoa)도 매우 강력합니다. 최신 제품이 전력 효율과 성능 면에서 압도적이기 때문입니다. 우리는 구형 제품에 매달리기보다 베니스(Venice)와 같은 차세대 제품으로 빠르게 전환하여 시장을 리드하는 데 집중하고 있습니다. 2027~2028년 수요를 미리 논의하며 공급망을 장기적으로 설계하고 있습니다.

[NEWS] German automakers are absorbing a bigger tariff hit:

Trump is raising EU auto tariffs from 15% to 25%, reversing a deal struck just last year.

Volkswagen Group already absorbed a $4.7 billion tariff hit in 2025.

Bernstein Research says the extra 10 points could cost German carmakers another $3.05 billion in operating profit.

Audi and Porsche U.S. retail sales already dropped 30% and 12%, respectively, in Q1 before this escalation.

The Takeaway: Import-heavy luxury brands could face higher costs AND softening demand.

Dealers carrying Audi or Porsche inventory should prepare for pricing pressure and potential supply shifts now.

Read today's top automotive stories, presented by WarrCloud: https://t.co/8XdRqBWoUL

(Source: Reuters / Bernstein Research)

tweets.text:
Evercore ISI 关于 GPU 与 ASIC / 光通信产业链的渠道调研：

到 2028 年，NVDA 在 AI 推理市场中的份额预计将下降至约 50%，原因是 AMD、Google TPU、Amazon Trainium、Microsoft Maia，以及基于 SRAM 的 AI 芯片持续进步。

“普通 AI 工程师越来越愿意采用 ASIC 或其他‘足够好（good enough）’的替代方案，以改善整体经济性（cost/performance）。”

NVIDIA B300 的交货周期（lead time）已从上季度的 12 周拉长至 12–16 周。

在 Rubin 架构时代，机柜内部（intra-rack）仍将主要使用铜互连；机柜之间（inter-rack）则会采用 CPO（共封装光学，Co-Packaged Optics）。而到了 Feynman 架构，机柜内部也将开始采用 CPO。

MRVL：AWS 在 2028 年对 6.4T NPO chiplet 的需求预计达到 1000 万颗。
ALAB：PCIe scale-up switch 的需求预计将持续增长至 2027 年。

tweets.text:
Everyone tracks the headline: USDT at roughly ~$184B vs USDC at ~$77B. Tether dominates on supply.

But the metric that actually reveals economic reality is adjusted transaction volume.

USDC: somewhere around ~$17-18 TRILLION in 2025.

USDT: roughly ~$12-13 TRILLION in 2025.

USDC handles significantly more economic activity with less than half the supply. That means USDC turns over its supply roughly 4x faster than USDT. The velocity difference reveals completely different use cases.

Year-to-date 2026, USDC holds around ~64% share of combined organic volume between the two, per Mizuho research. The gap is widening, not closing.

tweets.text:
Fintech Rundown (17/04) - @AmericanExpress, @Visa, @tempo, @X Money

Amex launches agentic commerce development kit. The kit will provide developers with access to agent verification services; account enablement; "intent intelligence" to ensure purchase intent is accurately captured; payment credentials to enable verified agents to complete payments; and cart context to support the sharing of cart details. Amex is also promising an industry-first commitment to offer customers protection for registered agent purchases. In the future, if a card member authorises an AI agent to make a purchase and that agent sends American Express the customer’s authenticated purchase intent, the firm will protect eligible customers from charges related to AI agent error.

Visa announced that it has launched a validator node on Tempo, a new Layer-1 blockchain optimized for agentic commerce and machine-to-machine payments. Visa is joining the Tempo network as an early validator, along with Stripe and Zodia Custody. Running a validator node gives the company deeper visibility into how blockchain-based payment systems function in real time. As a validator, Visa will earn rewards in stablecoins whenever it serves as the lead validator processing blocks. Increased blockchain payments are expected to enable Visa to capture more transaction volume and earn higher transaction fees.

X Money, the new add-on to the social media platform owned by Elon Musk, has the potential to disrupt the payments industry in the U.S. ��� including negatively impacting PayPal's business — Mizuho research analysts said on Thursday. However, New York State's recently introduced CRYPTO Act could complicate any future plans X Money may have for integrating crypto payments, they said. They also said the Clarity Act, which might end up limiting non-bank financial platforms' ability to offer customers yield, could hinder X Money's proposal to offer customers 6% annual percentage yield on cash balances.

That’s your fintech rundown. Stay tuned for more updates.

👉  Sponsor these conversations and get your brand in front of decision makers. DM me!

tweets.text:
ICYMI O/N (tgif hagw!)

MACRO:

The BoJ raised its benchmark interest rate by 25bps to 0.75% as highly anticipated and said more increases are in the pipeline if conditions allow. The decision was unanimous. JGB 10yr yields hit 2% after the decision. However, the Yen was little moved at the time and then saw weakening through Ueda's presser as the Governor gave little in the way of guidance for further rate hikes. (Newswires)

Japan’s Finance Minister Katayama said that communications with BoJ Governor Ueda have been very positive, with no gap in their thinking. (RTRS)

Japan’s Economy Minister Kiuchi said he respects the BoJ's decisions but stressed the need to remain mindful of the economic outlook. He emphasised the importance of close communication by the BoJ to clearly convey its thinking. (RTRS)

Japan's core consumer prices rose 3.0% in November from a year earlier, in line with the conesensus forecast, data showed on Friday, staying above the central bank's 2% target for the 44th straight month. (RTRS)

Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and her ruling coalition are poised to sign off on an income tax hike starting in 2027 to help finance a planned increase in defense spending, moving forward long-delayed plans as security concerns intensify. (BBG)

Japan’s Finance Minister Katayama said the government will consider fiscal sustainability to some extent when compiling next fiscal year’s budget and aims to boost market confidence by lowering the debt-to-GDP ratio. She also noted that the MoF had previously estimated a ¥400bn drop in tax revenue from lifting the income tax-free threshold, compared with a revised estimate of ¥650bn. (RTRS)

The BOJ's interest-rate hike to a three-decade high of 0.75% is likely a net positive for households — to the tune of about ¥800 billion ($5 billion) a year due to higher interest on savings, according to Mizuho Research & Technologies Ltd.’s senior economist. (BBG)

US President Trump signed into law new powers to screen and restrict US investment in Chinese tech firms, marking the most significant effort yet to police how American capital flows into businesses that bolster Beijing’s military and surveillance state. A spokesperson for the Chinese Embassy in Washington said the legislation was “overstretching the concept of national security” and would “distort normal investment flows between the two countries.” (WSJ)

TikTok has signed an agreement to create a US joint venture under a deal brokered by President Trump that leaves the app’s Chinese owner ByteDance with direct control of its core business operations in America. ByteDance will form a joint venture with Oracle, Silver Lake and Abu Dhabi’s MGX. (FT)

Overseas holdings of US Treasuries dipped in October as China sold down some of its stockpile while countries including Japan and the UK saw increases on the month. (BBG)

'The Data Problems in Thursday’s Inflation Report Will Linger for Months' WSJ article highlights the issues with the November CPI report. Missing October data means the BLS might not be able to calculate a Y/Y inflation rate in October 2026. Meanwhile,  housing costs, accounting for about one-third of the CPI, were affected by missing October rent data, which could skew inflation figures for six months. (WSJ)

'China boosts AI chip output by upgrading older ASML machines' FT sources article notes restricted chipmaking tools are being retrofitted to make advanced AI chips, exposing cracks in US-led export controls. (FT)

'PBOC’s Reverse Repos Fuel Expectations for Jan. RRR Cut' article in China Securities Journal citing analysts notes the PBoC's reverse repurchase agreements that started this week signals an effort to balance liquidity and steady market expectations ahead of the year-end. (China Securities Journal)

Global asset managers are facing a new roadblock in their struggle for a foothold in China’s market to sell fund products to wealthy investors. For the past half year, they’ve been unable to introduce any new products in a key program that helps such clients invest abroad. (BBG)

Inflows to a group of exchange-traded funds tracking the CSI A500 Index surged to a fresh record on Thursday, with the strong activity leaving traders perplexed. (BBG)

Chinese private funds are sticking with their long-term bets on AI and robotics shares, looking past a year-end selloff in tech sector, according to a report by the China Securities Journal. While long-term confidence remains high, firms are divided on how to handle the immediate pressure to sell, the report says. (China Securities Journal)

UK retail sales fell for a second straight month in November (-0.1% vs exp. +0.3%), adding to evidence that the economy was damaged by uncertainty in the run-up to Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves’ budget. (BBG)

UK government borrowing fell in November as the economy continued to generate healthy tax receipts and debt costs declined, a modest boost for Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves weeks after she unveiled a difficult budget. The deciit was still above analyst forecasts, however. (BBG)

Britons are more likely to buy big-ticket items than they were before Rachel Reeves’ budget, according to a GfK survey that suggests sentiment has improved in the run up to Christmas. (BBG)

The Bank of France raised its growth forecasts as the euro area’s second-largest economy shows resilience to prolonged political turbulence and fiscal uncertainty. (BBG)

Consumer confidence in New Zealand rose in December to its highest level in more than four years, with card spending data showing a lift in discretionary spending, ANZ-Roy Morgan data showed on Friday. (RTRS)

The Bank of Korea and the government took more measures to ease pressure on the won after an unscheduled board meeting, intensifying support for Asia’s worst-performing currency. (BBG)

GEOPOLITICAL:

European Union leaders agreed to loan Ukraine €90 billion ($106 billion) for the next two years in a bid to strengthen Kyiv’s hand at the negotiating table and keep the war-torn country afloat. (BBG)

French President Emmanuel Macron said Europe will have to find a way to directly engage with the Russian leader as the US pushes ahead with peace talks. (BBG)

A senior government official who advises Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on security expressed a personal opinion that Japan should have nuclear weapons, according to local media reports. (BBG)

EQUITIES:

WSJ sources reported OpenAI is aiming to raise as much as $100 billion as it seeks to pay for ambitious growth plans. The fundraising round could value the company at as much as $830 billion (The Information previously reported the round would be at a $750 billion valuation). The startup aims to complete the round by the end of the first quarter at the earliest. Terms of the deal could still change, and it is unclear whether there will be sufficient investor demand to reach the goal. (WSJ)

Nike made some progress with its turnaround plan in the fiscal second quarter, but expects to return to a sales decline in the current quarter, as weakness in China continues. Shares -10%. (WSJ)

FedEx posted higher revenue in its latest quarter, boosted an increase in package shipments in the U.S. and higher yields on goods across the globe. The results prompted the Memphis, Tenn., shipping company to raise the bottom end of its outlook for the full year, even as it navigates impacts from changes in global trade. Shares +0.5%. (WSJ)

Trump directed the federal government to reclassify cannabis as a less-dangerous drug, a boost to the legal marijuana industry. However, the move doesn’t legalize cannabis federally, and wouldn’t directly resolve banking challenges for marijuana businesses, something which cannabis investors had been hoping for. (WSJ)

Coinbase Global Inc. said it is suing the US states of Michigan, Illinois and Connecticut over their attempts to regulate prediction markets. (BBG)

California regulators reduced the profit that utilities are allowed to make on infrastructure investments in an effort to rein in soaring electricity bills. (BBG)

Wells Fargo achieved its highest-ever ranking on the U.S. M&A league tables, reaching seventh place from 14th last year. The bank’s investment banking growth is attributed to a strategic push under CEO Charlie Scharf, including hiring more than 90 managing directors. (WSJ)

Energy Transfer LP suspended development of a Louisiana liquefied natural gas export project to concentrate on building and expanding pipelines. (BBG)

Coty Inc. has sold its remaining stake in Wella to KKR & Co. for $750 million in cash and rights to certain future proceeds. (BBG)

Companies run by Tether’s secretive founder bought a crypto miner from an AI business owned by the stablecoin giant itself, highlighting how one of the world’s biggest crypto companies is managing its complex web of investments. (FT)

The administrator winding down what remains of Do Kwon’s Terraform Labs has sued Jump Trading, alleging that the high-speed trading giant unlawfully profited from and contributed to the crypto empire’s collapse. (WSJ) 

TSMC may only transfer abroad production technology 2-generations behind the current leading-edge due to Taiwan ‘National Core Technologies’ regulations, a deputy science minister told lawmakers who raised concerns about TSMC’s USA investment plans, media report. (CNA via @dnystedt) 

Toyota Motor said on Friday that it aims to begin selling its U.S.-made Camry sedan, Highlander SUV and Tundra pickup truck in Japan starting next year as it hopes to help improve Japan―U.S. trade relations. (RTRS)

Nidec Corp. said its founder Shigenobu Nagamori will step down from his position as chairman of the board, following the discovery of a series of accounting issues dating back years. (BBG)

Sony Group Corp.’s music and pictures units plan to spend around $460 million to take control of the company housing the classic Peanuts brand, created by Charles M. Schulz, expanding the Tokyo-based group’s reach in content. (BBG)

Japan’s ruling party has proposed cutting government subsidies for large-scale solar projects, as it pushes to reduce emphasis on renewables in the nation’s energy strategy. (BBG)

BBVA SA said it will carry out its largest share buyback ever as it seeks to draw a line under its failed bid for Banco Sabadell SA. (BBG)

ANZ Group will pay the largest combined penalties ever secured by Australia’s corporate regulator, over risk failures and widespread misconduct including in the handling of a US$9.26 billion government bond sale. (WSJ)

MACRO:

The BoJ raised its benchmark interest rate by 25bps to 0.75% as highly anticipated and said more increases are in the pipeline if conditions allow. The decision was unanimous. JGB 10yr yields hit 2% after the decision. However, the Yen was little moved at the time and then saw weakening through Ueda's presser as the Governor gave little in the way of guidance for further rate hikes. (Newswires)

Japan’s Finance Minister Katayama said that communications with BoJ Governor Ueda have been very positive, with no gap in their thinking. (RTRS)

Japan’s Economy Minister Kiuchi said he respects the BoJ's decisions but stressed the need to remain mindful of the economic outlook. He emphasised the importance of close communication by the BoJ to clearly convey its thinking. (RTRS)

Japan's core consumer prices rose 3.0% in November from a year earlier, in line with the conesensus forecast, data showed on Friday, staying above the central bank's 2% target for the 44th straight month. (RTRS)

Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and her ruling coalition are poised to sign off on an income tax hike starting in 2027 to help finance a planned increase in defense spending, moving forward long-delayed plans as security concerns intensify. (BBG)

Japan’s Finance Minister Katayama said the government will consider fiscal sustainability to some extent when compiling next fiscal year’s budget and aims to boost market confidence by lowering the debt-to-GDP ratio. She also noted that the MoF had previously estimated a ¥400bn drop in tax revenue from lifting the income tax-free threshold, compared with a revised estimate of ¥650bn. (RTRS)

The BOJ's interest-rate hike to a three-decade high of 0.75% is likely a net positive for households — to the tune of about ¥800 billion ($5 billion) a year due to higher interest on savings, according to Mizuho Research & Technologies Ltd.’s senior economist. (BBG)

US President Trump signed into law new powers to screen and restrict US investment in Chinese tech firms, marking the most significant effort yet to police how American capital flows into businesses that bolster Beijing’s military and surveillance state. A spokesperson for the Chinese Embassy in Washington said the legislation was “overstretching the concept of national security” and would “distort normal investment flows between the two countries.” (WSJ)

TikTok has signed an agreement to create a US joint venture under a deal brokered by President Trump that leaves the app’s Chinese owner ByteDance with direct control of its core business operations in America. ByteDance will form a joint venture with Oracle, Silver Lake and Abu Dhabi’s MGX. (FT)

Overseas holdings of US Treasuries dipped in October as China sold down some of its stockpile while countries including Japan and the UK saw increases on the month. (BBG)

'The Data Problems in Thursday’s Inflation Report Will Linger for Months' WSJ article highlights the issues with the November CPI report. Missing October data means the BLS might not be able to calculate a Y/Y inflation rate in October 2026. Meanwhile,  housing costs, accounting for about one-third of the CPI, were affected by missing October rent data, which could skew inflation figures for six months. (WSJ)

'China boosts AI chip output by upgrading older ASML machines' FT sources article notes restricted chipmaking tools are being retrofitted to make advanced AI chips, exposing cracks in US-led export controls. (FT)

'PBOC’s Reverse Repos Fuel Expectations for Jan. RRR Cut' article in China Securities Journal citing analysts notes the PBoC's reverse repurchase agreements that started this week signals an effort to balance liquidity and steady market expectations ahead of the year-end. (China Securities Journal)

Global asset managers are facing a new roadblock in their struggle for a foothold in China’s market to sell fund products to wealthy investors. For the past half year, they’ve been unable to introduce any new products in a key program that helps such clients invest abroad. (BBG)

Inflows to a group of exchange-traded funds tracking the CSI A500 Index surged to a fresh record on Thursday, with the strong activity leaving traders perplexed. (BBG)

Chinese private funds are sticking with their long-term bets on AI and robotics shares, looking past a year-end selloff in tech sector, according to a report by the China Securities Journal. While long-term confidence remains high, firms are divided on how to handle the immediate pressure to sell, the report says. (China Securities Journal)

tweets.text:
🔥 中国多地告急：万亿级大雷即将引爆！
日本《日经亚洲》11月18日最新报道，中国正在整合更多中小型金融机构，这些机构因向公共投资领域过度放贷、且缺乏还款前景而面临风险。

其中一个例子是，中国北部内蒙古自治区将120家较小的金融机构合并为内蒙古农村商业银行(Inner Mongolia Rural Commercial Bank)，该行已于今年5月开业。其位于自治区首府呼和浩特的新总部在周边建筑中格外醒目。

类似的新银行也在今年2月于内陆的河南省成立——这是由25家贷款机构合并而成。

农村金融机构加速整合：债务风险成主要推手

根据国家金融监督管理总局的数据，中国共有逾4,000家金融机构，但今年上半年数量减少了225家，降幅达5%，超过了2024年全年减少的195家。

被淘汰的机构大多位于农村地区，包括农业类贷款机构和村镇银行。这类银行约占中国全部金融机构的80%。

被清除出局的机构大多位于农村地区，包括农业类贷款机构和村镇银行。这类银行约占中国全部金融机构的80%。

随着经济低迷持续，农村人口外流使情况进一步恶化，加大了银行整合的压力。

今年早些时候，东北吉林省的13家金融机构被合并为一家农业银行。新疆维吾尔自治区也在推进类似的金融机构整合计划。

地方财政承压：房地产下行与预算外债务双重冲击

《日经亚洲》指出，新冠疫情以及中国房地产泡沫的破裂，也加深了地方政府的财政困境——这些政府曾被视为安全的借款方。

中国地方政府依赖土地财政，但随着房地产市场低迷，这一收入来源较2021年高峰已暴跌约60%。

预算外债务正成为地方政府的沉重负担。地方政府融资平台(LGFV)被用于公共工程及类似项目，但其中许多项目并不赚钱——例如鲜有人使用的高速公路——从而带来了违约风险，并可能对整个中国金融体系造成影响。

中国领导层在2024年决定在五年内投入10万亿元人民币(约合1.4万亿美元)，以控制此类隐性债务。

根据国际货币基金组织(IMF)估计，2023年中国地方政府融资平台的未偿债务达约60万亿元人民币。

日本综合研究所(Japan Research Institute)研究员Shinichi Seki指出：“目前的支持力度仍然不足。”

中共中央委员会在今年10月通过的《关于制定2030年前国家五年规划的建议》中表示：“要提高防范化解重点领域风险的能力，有序推进房地产、地方政府债务、中小金融机构等领域风险处置。”

尽管过去五年已获得总计5300亿元人民币的公共资金支持，但中国区域性银行仍累计拥有近1.4万亿元人民币的不良贷款。如果中国经济无法实现反弹，这一数字可能进一步膨胀。

区域性银行治理亟待加强：规模化并购难解根本问题

《日经亚洲》指出，银行合并能够形成规模效应，有望帮助稳定存续机构，但治理能力的提升同样不可或缺。

中国的区域性银行曾出现管理问题，包括地方监管部门违规干预等情况，有时甚至导致挤兑事件。

总部位于内蒙古的包商银行(Baoshang Bank)便是在类似背景下于2019年事实上被国有化，其近乎倒闭的原因被归咎于在主要股东授意下进行的鲁莽放贷。

瑞穗研究与技术公司(Mizuho Research& Technologies)首席经济学家Naoki Tsukioka表示：“对于中国金融体系的健康运行而言，最关键的是区域性银行要具备强有力的治理能力，并能够独立于相关利益方。”

Tsukioka指出，如果忽视治理问题，“提升地方经济、推动银行整合等举措的效果都会大打折扣。”

来源：FX168财经 
https://t.co/1hcpwKYrfz
tweets.full_text:
🔥 中国多地告急：万亿级大雷即将引爆！
日本《日经亚洲》11月18日最新报道，中国正在整合更多中小型金融机构，这些机构因向公共投资领域过度放贷、且缺乏还款前景而面临风险。

其中一个例子是，中国北部内蒙古自治区将120家较小的金融机构合并为内蒙古农村商业银行(Inner Mongolia Rural Commercial Bank)，该行已于今年5月开业。其位于自治区首府呼和浩特的新总部在周边建筑中格外醒目。

类似的新银行也在今年2月于内陆的河南省成立——这是由25家贷款机构合并而成。

农村金融机构加速整合：债务风险成主要推手

根据国家金融监督管理总局的数据，中国共有逾4,000家金融机构，但今年上半年数量减少了225家，降幅达5%，超过了2024年全年减少的195家。

被淘汰的机构大多位于农村地区，包括农业类贷款机构和村镇银行。这类银行约占中国全部金融机构的80%。

被清除出局的机构大多位于农村地区，包括农业类贷款机构和村镇银行。这类银行约占中国全部金融机构的80%。

随着经济低迷持续，农村人口外流使情况进一步恶化，加大了银行整合的压力。

今年早些时候，东北吉林省的13家金融机构被合并为一家农业银行。新疆维吾尔自治区也在推进类似的金融机构整合计划。

tweets.text:
Warum Institutionen trotz allem bullish auf Crypto bleiben

Ich höre immer wieder die Frage, warum große Institutionen trotz Marktvolatilität weiterhin in Krypto investieren. Die Antwort hat sich in den letzten zwei Jahren fundamental verändert. Es geht nicht mehr um Spekulation. Es geht um Infrastruktur.

Real World Assets sind der erste konkrete Hebel. Tokenisierte reale Vermögenswerte haben die 30-Milliarden-Dollar-Marke überschritten, getrieben von Private Credit und US-Staatsanleihen on-chain. BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, Goldman Sachs, BNY Mellon: Namen, die vor fünf Jahren niemand mit DeFi in einem Satz nennen würde. BCG schätzt das Marktpotenzial auf bis zu 16 Billionen Dollar bis 2030, Standard Chartered sogar auf 30 Billionen bis 2034. Das sind keine Hochglanzprognosen. Das sind Projektionen auf Basis von Kapitalflüssen, die bereits heute stattfinden.

Dazu kommt die regulatorische Klarheit, die jahrelang gefehlt hat. Der GENIUS Act hat im Juli 2025 erstmals einen bundesweiten Rahmen für Stablecoins in den USA geschaffen. Europas MiCA ist in Kraft. Und der CLARITY Act, der die Zuständigkeiten zwischen SEC und CFTC für digitale Assets klären soll, wird gerade im US-Senat vorangetrieben. Über 66 Prozent der institutionellen Anleger nennen regulatorische Fortschritte als entscheidenden Faktor für ihre wachsende Crypto-Allokation. Das ist keine Stimmung, das sind Umfragedaten.

Stablecoins sind dabei leise zu einem der wichtigsten Infrastruktur-Layer geworden. Die Marktkapitalisierung überschreitet 300 Milliarden Dollar. Allein Tether hält über 127 Milliarden Dollar in US-Staatsanleihen, mehr als viele Nationalstaaten. Banken wie JPMorgan und BNY Mellon bereiten eigene Stablecoin-Produkte vor. Stablecoins sind nicht mehr Werkzeug für Trader. Sie werden zu Settlement-Infrastruktur im institutionellen Zahlungsverkehr, und wer das noch bezweifelt, sollte sich die Transaktionsvolumina ansehen, die mittlerweile an Visa-Dimensionen heranreichen.

Bitcoin hat sich in diesem Kontext neu positioniert. CME Bitcoin Futures Open Interest erreichte kürzlich ein Allzeithoch bei 45 Milliarden Dollar. Spot-ETFs halten inzwischen über 1,36 Millionen BTC, rund 7 Prozent des Gesamtumlaufs. BlackRocks IBIT allein verwaltet Bitcoin-Bestände im Wert von rund 63 Milliarden Dollar. Das lässt sich nicht mit Zockerei erklären. Bitcoin wird als makrostrategisches Asset behandelt, vergleichbar mit Gold, aber mit überlegener Settlement-Effizienz.

Der übergeordnete Punkt: Institutionen behandeln digitale Assets zunehmend als operative Infrastruktur, nicht als Randposition im Portfolio. Das bedeutet nicht, dass alle Risiken verschwunden sind. Regulierung bleibt global fragmentiert, Interoperabilität zwischen Blockchains ist ungelöst, und viele tokenisierte Assets leiden unter geringer Sekundärmarktliquidität. Aber die Richtung ist eindeutig. Wer das als kurzfristigen Hype abtut, sollte sich die Kapitalflüsse genauer ansehen.

Quellen: Investax Q3 2025 RWA Tokenization Report, BCG / ADDX Asset Tokenization Report, Standard Chartered Research, RedStone / https://t.co/kaRallgHPJ / Gauntlet "Real-World Assets in On-chain Finance Report" (2025), StealthEx Crypto Market Review 2025, CoinReporter (April 2026), TRM Labs 2025 Crypto Adoption & Stablecoin Usage Report

tweets.text: Demands for zero illicit exposure ignore how permissionless systems operate. Risk-based models matter more than purity tests. Platforms like Binance,  operate under constant scrutiny, where speed of detection and recovery define resilience. Standard Chartered⁠� research highlights systemic risk as inherent, not exceptional.

tweets.text:
.@binance is finally addressing the elephant in the room. Demanding zero illicit exposure on a blockchain is a technical trap. Regulators are pushing for a standard that does not exist even in the 2T$ legacy banking world. This is not about a lack of effort but the actual nature of a permissionless ledger.

As the Standard Chartered research noted after the 300M$ DeFi rescue last month, systemic risk is part of the architecture. If the bar for compliance is absolute perfection, the industry is being set up for guaranteed failure. We need to move toward a risk based model that accounts for the 99% of legitimate users rather than choking the system over the 1% of bad actors. Realism is the only way forward for regulation in 2026.

The 2023 settlement with the DOJ was supposed to be a turning point, but the current tension shows that monitors and tech can only go so far. When you operate at the scale of Binance, you are essentially a lightning rod for every global financial friction point. Success should be measured by the speed of the response and the strength of the recovery, not an impossible 100% purity test.

https://t.co/DoseB5plAO

tweets.text:
🏛 White House Digital Assets coordinator defends stablecoin yields

Patrick Witt, Executive Director of the White House Council of Advisers on Digital Assets, has pushed back against traditional banking sector concerns over stablecoin yields, arguing that compliant stablecoins will bring net new capital into the American banking system rather than drain existing deposits.

Speaking via a post on X, Witt stated that foreign demand for dollar-denominated stablecoins issued by U.S.-based issuers represents an inflow of capital, not a diversion of domestic deposits. Most U.S. stablecoin issuers hold U.S. dollars or Treasury securities as collateral backing each token issued.

His remarks come amid growing tensions between the crypto industry and traditional finance over the U.S. CLARITY Act.

His position contrasts with a recent Standard Chartered research note, which estimated that rising stablecoin adoption could reduce U.S. bank deposits by an amount equal to one third of the stablecoin market capitalization.

https://t.co/pvVo3ttT6U

Standard Chartered research warns that accelerating stablecoin adoption could drive significant deposit outflows from banks in developed economies, with as much as $500 billion potentially shifting into digital assets by 2028, including roughly one-third of stablecoin market capitalization from U.S. bank deposits. 

The stablecoin supply has already expanded by about 40% year over year to over $300 billion and may accelerate further as crypto legislation advances.

Arthur Hayes discussed potential liquidity injections driven by pressure on the Japanese yen and JGB market.

tweets.text:
**DAILY CRYPTO MARKET ANALYSIS - OCTOBER 7, 2025**

---

**1) MARKET OVERVIEW**

**Bitcoin:** $122,111 (-2.14% from yesterday's ATH of $126,279)
**Ethereum:** $4,496 (-4.05% from $4,693)
**Total Crypto Market Cap:** $4.36 trillion (+1% from yesterday)
**24-Hour Trading Volume:** $198 billion
**Fear & Greed Index:** 70 (Greed) - down from 71 yesterday

Source: Coinbase, Yahoo Finance, https://t.co/4s9RuPSSzO

**MY TAKE:** Bitcoin's 3% pullback from Monday's record high is healthy profit-taking after a parabolic run, NOT a trend reversal. The Fear & Greed Index holding at 70 shows greed remains controlled—we're not seeing the euphoric 90+ readings that mark cycle tops. I'm 75% confident this dip to $122K represents a buying opportunity before the next leg to $135K-$145K by year-end.

---

**2) MAJOR NEWS SPOTLIGHT**

**Bitcoin ETFs Shatter Records: $1.21 Billion Single-Day Inflows**

On October 6, 2025, US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $1.21 billion in net inflows—the second-largest daily haul in history, only beaten by the $1.37 billion on November 7, 2024 (the day after Trump's election victory). BlackRock's IBIT dominated with $970 million alone, followed by Fidelity's FBTC at $112.3 million.

Source: The Block, CCN

**BlackRock's IBIT Approaches Historic $100 Billion Milestone**

IBIT now holds approximately 791,628 BTC worth $98.31 billion, making it BlackRock's most profitable ETF by annual revenue—surpassing even its S&P 500 fund. IBIT is on track to become the fastest ETF ever to hit $100 billion in assets under management, achieving this in just 435 days versus VOO's previous record of 2,011 days. Only 18 of 4,500+ ETFs have exceeded $100 billion AUM in the 30+ year history of ETFs.

Source: The Block, Bloomberg analysis

**Grayscale Launches First US Staking ETFs**

On October 6, Grayscale debuted the first US-listed spot crypto ETFs offering staking rewards: Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHE) and Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH). Within the first day, Grayscale staked 32,000 ETH (~$150 million), marking a significant evolution in crypto ETF products.

Source: ETF Express, Cointelegraph

**MY OPINION:** This changes everything. The $1.21 billion single-day inflow isn't noise—it's Wall Street voting with real capital. When institutions deploy this kind of firepower AFTER Bitcoin already hit $126K, they're signaling conviction that much higher prices are coming. This isn't retail FOMO; it's calculated institutional positioning.

What most analysts miss: BlackRock's IBIT earning more revenue than their S&P 500 fund reveals how lucrative crypto has become for traditional finance. This creates a powerful incentive structure—every major financial institution will now race to capture crypto market share, driving a multi-year adoption wave.

**MY ANALYSIS - Short & Long-Term Implications:**

**Short-term (Next 30 days):** The $1.21B inflow creates immediate buying pressure absorbing 9,760 BTC (at $124K/BTC) against daily issuance of only ~450 BTC from mining. This 22:1 demand-to-supply ratio mathematically supports $130K-$135K by month-end. The 3% pullback to $122K offers tactical entry.

**Long-term (12-18 months):** Standard Chartered's $200K year-end target and $20 billion additional ETF inflows projection now looks conservative. If IBIT alone maintains $1B weekly inflows, that's $52B annually—enough to absorb the entire annual Bitcoin supply twice over. Combined with the 21 million hard cap and increasing institutional adoption, I'm establishing a 12-month price target of $175K-$200K with 70% confidence.

**Challenge to consensus:** Most analysts cite "overbought" conditions as bearish. I disagree. In commodity markets with fixed supply and accelerating institutional demand, traditional technical indicators break down. Bitcoin's RSI at 68-70 can persist for months during structural bull markets driven by ETF adoption—exactly what happened with gold ETFs in 2004-2011 when GLD launched and gold rose from $400 to $1,900.

---

**3) TOP MOVERS**

**Top 3 Gainers:**

1. **BNB:** +6.88% to $1,307 (NEW ALL-TIME HIGH)
   - **Reason:** BNB Chain recorded 58 million monthly active addresses, surpassing Solana's 38.3 million. Decentralized exchange Aster drove growth with TVL surging 500% to $2.4 billion. BNB Chain also partnered with Chainlink to bring official US economic data on-chain.
   - **Source:** CoinDesk, BeInCrypto

2. **Ethereum:** +4.02% to $4,497
   - **Reason:** Grayscale's staking ETF launch staked $150M on day one, validating institutional appetite for yield-generating crypto products. ETH ETFs saw $181.8 million inflows on October 6.
   - **Source:** The Block

3. **Avalanche (AVAX):** +7.92% to $28.19
   - **Reason:** Benefiting from broader altcoin rotation as Bitcoin dominance dropped below 59%.
   - **Source:** Market data from CoinGecko

**Top 3 Losers:**

1. **Solana (SOL):** -4.20% to $222
   - **Reason:** Despite strong fundamentals, short-term profit-taking after recent rally. However, $59 billion in combined XRP and Solana futures volume indicates sustained institutional interest.
   - **Source:** TradingView

2. **XRP:** -0.9% to $2.97
   - **Reason:** Retail sentiment flipped bearish (contrarian buy signal per analyst reports). Technical consolidation below $3 resistance.
   - **Source:** CoinDesk market data

3. **Chainlink (LINK):** -1.93% to $22.06
   - **Reason:** Temporary pullback despite positive BNB Chain partnership news. Market-wide profit-taking in DeFi tokens.

**Notable On-Chain Metrics:**

- **Bitcoin Whale Activity:** 190,000 BTC accumulated near $117K support level provides strong floor. Cost basis distribution shows thin support at $120-121K but massive demand at $117K.
- **Ethereum Whale Movement:** Large whale deposited 15,000 ETH ($70M) to Bitfinex, total 30,000 ETH ($138M) moved to exchanges in 48 hours signals potential distribution.
- **Liquidations:** $357 million in total liquidations over 24 hours (145,000 traders liquidated), with long positions bearing most losses during the Bitcoin pullback.

Sources: Glassnode, Coinglass

**MY TAKE ON MOVES:**

**BNB's 22% weekly surge is justified.** The 58 million active address milestone and Aster's 500% TVL growth represent real adoption metrics, not speculation. However, on-chain data shows 4 million BNB ($4.8 billion) moved to exchanges in 48 hours—classic profit-taking setup. RSI above 70 signals short-term overextension. I'd take 25-30% profits here and look to re-enter at $1,150-$1,180 support.

**Ethereum's move is underrated.** While up only 4%, the Grayscale staking ETF staking $150M on day one validates what I've been saying: institutions want yield-generating crypto exposure. ETH's 56% discount to its all-time high versus BTC's new highs creates asymmetric upside. Target: $5,200-$5,800 (19-33% upside) within 60 days.

**Solana and XRP weakness is overreaction.** The $59 billion futures volume for both combined shows institutional positioning for ETF approvals. XRP's bearish retail sentiment is actually a contrarian buy signal—retail is always wrong at extremes. Both are coiling for breakouts once Bitcoin stabilizes above $125K.

---

**4) MARKET SCENARIOS & MY OUTLOOK**

**Bullish Case: $135K-$150K by Year-End (65% Probability)**

**Catalysts:**
- Continued $3B+ weekly ETF inflows retiring 100,000+ BTC quarterly (double new issuance)
- BlackRock IBIT crossing $100B AUM milestone attracts copycat flows from other institutions
- Federal Reserve maintains dovish stance; October typically sees 15-20% Bitcoin gains historically
- US government shutdown paradoxically bullish—fiscal instability drives "debasement trade" into hard assets
- Standard Chartered's $20B additional inflow projection for 2025 materializing
- Corporate treasury adoption accelerating

**Technical Support:**
- Key support: $122K-$123K (current), $117K (massive 190K BTC accumulated)
- Resistance: $126,300 (ATH), $128K-$130K, then $135K
- 50-day MA: $113,771 rising, 200-day MA: $104,804 rising (both bullish)
- RSI at 68-70 shows room for continuation in strong trends

**Bearish Case: $105K-$118K Correction (25% Probability)**

**Risks:**
- Overleveraged long positions ($187 billion open interest) vulnerable to cascading liquidations
- $357M liquidations in 24 hours shows fragility; further $2B potential if $118K breaks
- BlackRock profit-taking if IBIT crosses $100B (psychological milestone)
- VIX at 17.24 (+5.31%) indicates rising macro volatility could spill into crypto
- Government shutdown extending beyond October 15 payroll deadline creates real economic damage
- Traditional market correction (S&P 500 barely holding 50-day MA at 6,650) dragging crypto lower
- Ethereum whale movements ($138M to exchanges) suggests smart money distributing

**Technical Breakdown Levels:**
- Break below $120K triggers sell to $117K (190K BTC buy wall)
- $117K failure cascades to $110K (50-day MA)
- Full correction to $105K possible but would represent healthy 18% drawdown

**MY CONVICTION: Most Likely Scenario (65% Confidence)**

**Base Case: Consolidation $122K-$126K (2-3 weeks) then breakout to $135K-$145K by November**

Here's why I believe this with strong conviction:

**Mathematical Reality:** Daily Bitcoin issuance = ~450 BTC (~$56M at $124K). Weekly ETF inflows = $3B+ = absorbing 24,000 BTC. This creates 53:1 weekly demand-to-supply ratio. No asset can sustain this imbalance without repricing significantly higher. Even with 50% inflow reduction, we're still at 27:1 ratio—bullish.

**Historical Pattern:** October 2023 saw similar setup—Bitcoin broke $30K in early October, consolidated 2 weeks, then ran to $45K by December. October 2024: broke $70K, consolidated, ran to $109K by January 2025. The pattern: "Uptober" launches rallies that peak in Q4/Q1.

**Institutional Behavior:** When BlackRock's IBIT generates more revenue than their S&P 500 ETF, every competitor (Fidelity, Vanguard, State Street) must respond. This creates a multi-year adoption S-curve we're only 12% through (based on ETF AUM vs. total financial assets). First-mover advantage forces followers to buy regardless of price.

**Contrarian Indicator:** Everyone citing "overbought RSI" and "overheated rally" is fighting the tape. Smart money doesn't care about RSI—they care about supply/demand fundamentals. When institutions absorb 53x daily supply, RSI can stay "overbought" for months (see gold 2010-2011, tech stocks 2023-2024).

**MY RECOMMENDED POSITIONING:**

**For Bitcoin Holders (50%+ of portfolio):**
- HOLD all positions above $120K
- ADD 10-15% on any dip to $118K-$120K range
- Set stops only below $115K (would invalidate thesis)
- Target: $135K (take 30% profits), $150K (take another 30%), hold rest for $175K+

**For Ethereum Positions (20-25%):**
- ACCUMULATE aggressively below $4,500
- Grayscale staking ETF is game-changer; institutions want yield
- 56% below ATH vs. BTC at ATH = asymmetric risk/reward
- Target: $5,200 (take 25% profits), $5,800 (take 25%), hold rest for $7,000+

**For Altcoin Exposure (15-20%):**
- BNB: Take 30% profits above $1,250, reload at $1,150-$1,180
- Solana: BUY below $220, Target $280-$300 (ETF approval catalyst)
- XRP: ACCUMULATE $2.85-$3.00 (contrarian play), Target $3.40-$3.70
- Avoid: Small caps with 400%+ recent pumps—these are distribution zones

**For Stablecoins (10-15%):**
- Keep dry powder for $117K-$120K dip that may not come
- If BTC breaks $128K, deploy 50% of stables immediately
- Never go below 5% stablecoins (volatility insurance)

**Key Support/Resistance Levels to Watch (Next 48-72 Hours):**

**Bitcoin:**
- Immediate support: $122K-$123K (MUST HOLD)
- Critical support: $120K (psychological), $117K (190K BTC accumulation)
- Resistance: $126,300 (ATH), $128K, $130K, then $135K
- Invalidation: Close below $117K = expect $110K-$105K correction

**Ethereum:**
- Support: $4,500 (current), $4,300-$4,400 (secondary)
- Resistance: $4,700-$4,750, then $4,850, breakthrough to $5,000+
- Bullish above: $4,500 daily close
- Bearish below: $4,300 (tests $4,000-$4,100)

---

**5) REGULATORY & INSTITUTIONAL UPDATES**

**US Government Shutdown Impact (Week 2)**

The partial government shutdown, now in its second week, has created an unexpected tailwind for Bitcoin. Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick reaffirmed his $200,000 year-end Bitcoin price target, citing the shutdown as driving "debasement trades" into hard assets like gold and BTC.

Source: The Block, Standard Chartered research

**SEC Regulatory Progress**

SEC Chair Atkins announced plans to formalize the "Innovation Exemption" by year-end, potentially streamlining crypto product approvals. However, the government shutdown has delayed decisions on Litecoin, Hedera (HBAR), Solana, and XRP ETF applications.

Source: The Block

**S&P Global Index Launch**

S&P Global announced the S&P Digital Markets 50 Index, combining 35 crypto-linked equities with 15 cryptocurrencies. Dinari will create a token tracking the benchmark, marking a significant bridge between traditional finance and crypto markets.

Source: S&P Global Press Release

**MY OPINION: Real Impact vs. Noise**

**Real Impact:**
- Government shutdown is BULLISH short-term (fiscal instability narrative) but could turn bearish if extending beyond October 15
- SEC's "Innovation Exemption" formalization by year-end could unlock $50B+ in new crypto product launches
- S&P Digital Markets 50 Index legitimizes crypto-equity hybrids, opening $100B+ in index fund capital

**Noise:**
- Daily shutdown headlines—markets already priced this in
- Generic "regulatory uncertainty" concerns—we've had 5+ years of this, institutions keep buying

The key insight: Traditional finance is building permanent infrastructure around crypto (ETFs, indices, custody solutions). Once built, this infrastructure becomes self-reinforcing regardless of short-term regulatory headlines.

---

**6) ACTIONABLE INSIGHTS & MY RECOMMENDATIONS**

**What I Would Be Doing in This Market:**

**My Actual Positioning (Hypothetical $100K Portfolio):**
- 45% Bitcoin (HOLD, waiting for $118K dip to add)
- 20% Ethereum (ACCUMULATING $4,400-$4,500 aggressively)
- 10% Solana (building position $220-$230 for ETF catalyst)
- 8% BNB (took 30% profits at $1,250, holding rest)
- 5% XRP (contrarian bet $2.85-$3.00)
- 12% USDC (dry powder for volatility)

**Key Levels & Events to Monitor (Next 24-48 Hours):**
- Bitcoin $122K support (CRITICAL)
- Ethereum $4,500 support (ACCUMULATION ZONE)
- Thursday: ETF inflow data
- VIX movements above 18

**Risk Factors I Think Are Most Important:**

1. **Bitcoin Breaking $120K Support (30% probability, HIGH impact)**
2. **VIX Spiking Above 20 (25% probability, MEDIUM impact)**
3. **Government Shutdown Past Oct 15 (40% probability, MEDIUM impact)**

**ONE CONTRARIAN VIEW:**

**Popular Belief:** "Bitcoin is overbought and needs a 20% correction to $100K. RSI above 70 is a sell signal."

**My Contrarian Take:** This is wrong. When daily demand exceeds supply by 53:1, RSI becomes meaningless. Gold stayed "overbought" (RSI >70) for most of 2005-2007 as it rose from $400 to $1,000 during ETF adoption. The real risk isn't buying "overbought" Bitcoin at $122K—it's sitting in cash waiting for a $100K correction that never comes.

**My Conviction:** I'm 70% confident Bitcoin never sees sub-$110K again this cycle. The ETF structure creates permanent buying pressure that overwhelms any correction attempt.

---

**BOTTOM LINE:**

Bitcoin's pullback to $122K represents tactical opportunity, not trend change. Record $1.21B daily ETF inflows demonstrate institutional conviction AFTER new highs—bullish continuation signal. BlackRock's IBIT approaching $100B AUM milestone validates crypto as mainstream asset class.

**Highest Conviction Calls:**
1. Bitcoin $135K-$145K by November (65% confidence)
2. Ethereum $5,200-$5,800 by December (70% confidence)
3. Altcoin season confirmed if BTC holds $120K (75% confidence)
4. BNB retraces to $1,150-$1,180 before resuming (60% confidence)

**The market has spoken: $1.21 billion doesn't flow into "overbought" assets—it flows into assets institutions believe are undervalued. Position accordingly.**

---

Data verified through: Coinbase, YCharts, The Block, CoinDesk, BeInCrypto, Glassnode, CryptoQuant, CoinGecko, TradingView, https://t.co/4s9RuPSSzO

Report Timestamp: October 7, 2025, 6:00 PM UTC

IBIT now holds approximately 791,628 BTC worth $98.31 billion, making it BlackRock's most profitable ETF by annual revenue—surpassing even its S&P 500 fund. IBIT is on track to become the fastest ETF ever to hit $100 billion in assets under management, achieving this in just 435 days versus VOO's previous record of 2,011 days. Only 18 of 4,500+ ETFs have exceeded $100 billion AUM in the 30+ year history of ETFs.

Source: The Block, Bloomberg analysis

**Grayscale Launches First US Staking ETFs**

On October 6, Grayscale debuted the first US-listed spot crypto ETFs offering staking rewards: Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHE) and Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH). Within the first day, Grayscale staked 32,000 ETH (~$150 million), marking a significant evolution in crypto ETF products.

Source: ETF Express, Cointelegraph

**MY OPINION:** This changes everything. The $1.21 billion single-day inflow isn't noise—it's Wall Street voting with real capital. When institutions deploy this kind of firepower AFTER Bitcoin already hit $126K, they're signaling conviction that much higher prices are coming. This isn't retail FOMO; it's calculated institutional positioning.

What most analysts miss: BlackRock's IBIT earning more revenue than their S&P 500 fund reveals how lucrative crypto has become for traditional finance. This creates a powerful incentive structure—every major financial institution will now race to capture crypto market share, driving a multi-year adoption wave.

**MY ANALYSIS - Short & Long-Term Implications:**

**Short-term (Next 30 days):** The $1.21B inflow creates immediate buying pressure absorbing 9,760 BTC (at $124K/BTC) against daily issuance of only ~450 BTC from mining. This 22:1 demand-to-supply ratio mathematically supports $130K-$135K by month-end. The 3% pullback to $122K offers tactical entry.

**Long-term (12-18 months):** Standard Chartered's $200K year-end target and $20 billion additional ETF inflows projection now looks conservative. If IBIT alone maintains $1B weekly inflows, that's $52B annually—enough to absorb the entire annual Bitcoin supply twice over. Combined with the 21 million hard cap and increasing institutional adoption, I'm establishing a 12-month price target of $175K-$200K with 70% confidence.

**Challenge to consensus:** Most analysts cite "overbought" conditions as bearish. I disagree. In commodity markets with fixed supply and accelerating institutional demand, traditional technical indicators break down. Bitcoin's RSI at 68-70 can persist for months during structural bull markets driven by ETF adoption—exactly what happened with gold ETFs in 2004-2011 when GLD launched and gold rose from $400 to $1,900.

---

**3) TOP MOVERS**

**Top 3 Gainers:**

1. **BNB:** +6.88% to $1,307 (NEW ALL-TIME HIGH)
   - **Reason:** BNB Chain recorded 58 million monthly active addresses, surpassing Solana's 38.3 million. Decentralized exchange Aster drove growth with TVL surging 500% to $2.4 billion. BNB Chain also partnered with Chainlink to bring official US economic data on-chain.
   - **Source:** CoinDesk, BeInCrypto

2. **Ethereum:** +4.02% to $4,497
   - **Reason:** Grayscale's staking ETF launch staked $150M on day one, validating institutional appetite for yield-generating crypto products. ETH ETFs saw $181.8 million inflows on October 6.
   - **Source:** The Block

3. **Avalanche (AVAX):** +7.92% to $28.19
   - **Reason:** Benefiting from broader altcoin rotation as Bitcoin dominance dropped below 59%.
   - **Source:** Market data from CoinGecko

**Top 3 Losers:**

1. **Solana (SOL):** -4.20% to $222
   - **Reason:** Despite strong fundamentals, short-term profit-taking after recent rally. However, $59 billion in combined XRP and Solana futures volume indicates sustained institutional interest.
   - **Source:** TradingView

2. **XRP:** -0.9% to $2.97
   - **Reason:** Retail sentiment flipped bearish (contrarian buy signal per analyst reports). Technical consolidation below $3 resistance.
   - **Source:** CoinDesk market data

3. **Chainlink (LINK):** -1.93% to $22.06
   - **Reason:** Temporary pullback despite positive BNB Chain partnership news. Market-wide profit-taking in DeFi tokens.

**Notable On-Chain Metrics:**

- **Bitcoin Whale Activity:** 190,000 BTC accumulated near $117K support level provides strong floor. Cost basis distribution shows thin support at $120-121K but massive demand at $117K.
- **Ethereum Whale Movement:** Large whale deposited 15,000 ETH ($70M) to Bitfinex, total 30,000 ETH ($138M) moved to exchanges in 48 hours signals potential distribution.
- **Liquidations:** $357 million in total liquidations over 24 hours (145,000 traders liquidated), with long positions bearing most losses during the Bitcoin pullback.

Sources: Glassnode, Coinglass

**MY TAKE ON MOVES:**

**BNB's 22% weekly surge is justified.** The 58 million active address milestone and Aster's 500% TVL growth represent real adoption metrics, not speculation. However, on-chain data shows 4 million BNB ($4.8 billion) moved to exchanges in 48 hours—classic profit-taking setup. RSI above 70 signals short-term overextension. I'd take 25-30% profits here and look to re-enter at $1,150-$1,180 support.

**Ethereum's move is underrated.** While up only 4%, the Grayscale staking ETF staking $150M on day one validates what I've been saying: institutions want yield-generating crypto exposure. ETH's 56% discount to its all-time high versus BTC's new highs creates asymmetric upside. Target: $5,200-$5,800 (19-33% upside) within 60 days.

**Solana and XRP weakness is overreaction.** The $59 billion futures volume for both combined shows institutional positioning for ETF approvals. XRP's bearish retail sentiment is actually a contrarian buy signal—retail is always wrong at extremes. Both are coiling for breakouts once Bitcoin stabilizes above $125K.

---

**4) MARKET SCENARIOS & MY OUTLOOK**

**Bullish Case: $135K-$150K by Year-End (65% Probability)**

**Catalysts:**
- Continued $3B+ weekly ETF inflows retiring 100,000+ BTC quarterly (double new issuance)
- BlackRock IBIT crossing $100B AUM milestone attracts copycat flows from other institutions
- Federal Reserve maintains dovish stance; October typically sees 15-20% Bitcoin gains historically
- US government shutdown paradoxically bullish—fiscal instability drives "debasement trade" into hard assets
- Standard Chartered's $20B additional inflow projection for 2025 materializing
- Corporate treasury adoption accelerating

**Technical Support:**
- Key support: $122K-$123K (current), $117K (massive 190K BTC accumulated)
- Resistance: $126,300 (ATH), $128K-$130K, then $135K
- 50-day MA: $113,771 rising, 200-day MA: $104,804 rising (both bullish)
- RSI at 68-70 shows room for continuation in strong trends

**Bearish Case: $105K-$118K Correction (25% Probability)**

**Risks:**
- Overleveraged long positions ($187 billion open interest) vulnerable to cascading liquidations
- $357M liquidations in 24 hours shows fragility; further $2B potential if $118K breaks
- BlackRock profit-taking if IBIT crosses $100B (psychological milestone)
- VIX at 17.24 (+5.31%) indicates rising macro volatility could spill into crypto
- Government shutdown extending beyond October 15 payroll deadline creates real economic damage
- Traditional market correction (S&P 500 barely holding 50-day MA at 6,650) dragging crypto lower
- Ethereum whale movements ($138M to exchanges) suggests smart money distributing

**Technical Breakdown Levels:**
- Break below $120K triggers sell to $117K (190K BTC buy wall)
- $117K failure cascades to $110K (50-day MA)
- Full correction to $105K possible but would represent healthy 18% drawdown

**MY CONVICTION: Most Likely Scenario (65% Confidence)**

**Base Case: Consolidation $122K-$126K (2-3 weeks) then breakout to $135K-$145K by November**

Here's why I believe this with strong conviction:

**Mathematical Reality:** Daily Bitcoin issuance = ~450 BTC (~$56M at $124K). Weekly ETF inflows = $3B+ = absorbing 24,000 BTC. This creates 53:1 weekly demand-to-supply ratio. No asset can sustain this imbalance without repricing significantly higher. Even with 50% inflow reduction, we're still at 27:1 ratio—bullish.

**Historical Pattern:** October 2023 saw similar setup—Bitcoin broke $30K in early October, consolidated 2 weeks, then ran to $45K by December. October 2024: broke $70K, consolidated, ran to $109K by January 2025. The pattern: "Uptober" launches rallies that peak in Q4/Q1.

**Institutional Behavior:** When BlackRock's IBIT generates more revenue than their S&P 500 ETF, every competitor (Fidelity, Vanguard, State Street) must respond. This creates a multi-year adoption S-curve we're only 12% through (based on ETF AUM vs. total financial assets). First-mover advantage forces followers to buy regardless of price.

**Contrarian Indicator:** Everyone citing "overbought RSI" and "overheated rally" is fighting the tape. Smart money doesn't care about RSI—they care about supply/demand fundamentals. When institutions absorb 53x daily supply, RSI can stay "overbought" for months (see gold 2010-2011, tech stocks 2023-2024).

**MY RECOMMENDED POSITIONING:**

**For Bitcoin Holders (50%+ of portfolio):**
- HOLD all positions above $120K
- ADD 10-15% on any dip to $118K-$120K range
- Set stops only below $115K (would invalidate thesis)
- Target: $135K (take 30% profits), $150K (take another 30%), hold rest for $175K+

**For Ethereum Positions (20-25%):**
- ACCUMULATE aggressively below $4,500
- Grayscale staking ETF is game-changer; institutions want yield
- 56% below ATH vs. BTC at ATH = asymmetric risk/reward
- Target: $5,200 (take 25% profits), $5,800 (take 25%), hold rest for $7,000+

**For Altcoin Exposure (15-20%):**
- BNB: Take 30% profits above $1,250, reload at $1,150-$1,180
- Solana: BUY below $220, Target $280-$300 (ETF approval catalyst)
- XRP: ACCUMULATE $2.85-$3.00 (contrarian play), Target $3.40-$3.70
- Avoid: Small caps with 400%+ recent pumps—these are distribution zones

**For Stablecoins (10-15%):**
- Keep dry powder for $117K-$120K dip that may not come
- If BTC breaks $128K, deploy 50% of stables immediately
- Never go below 5% stablecoins (volatility insurance)

**Key Support/Resistance Levels to Watch (Next 48-72 Hours):**

**Bitcoin:**
- Immediate support: $122K-$123K (MUST HOLD)
- Critical support: $120K (psychological), $117K (190K BTC accumulation)
- Resistance: $126,300 (ATH), $128K, $130K, then $135K
- Invalidation: Close below $117K = expect $110K-$105K correction

**Ethereum:**
- Support: $4,500 (current), $4,300-$4,400 (secondary)
- Resistance: $4,700-$4,750, then $4,850, breakthrough to $5,000+
- Bullish above: $4,500 daily close
- Bearish below: $4,300 (tests $4,000-$4,100)

---

**5) REGULATORY & INSTITUTIONAL UPDATES**

**US Government Shutdown Impact (Week 2)**

The partial government shutdown, now in its second week, has created an unexpected tailwind for Bitcoin. Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick reaffirmed his $200,000 year-end Bitcoin price target, citing the shutdown as driving "debasement trades" into hard assets like gold and BTC.

Source: The Block, Standard Chartered research

**SEC Regulatory Progress**

SEC Chair Atkins announced plans to formalize the "Innovation Exemption" by year-end, potentially streamlining crypto product approvals. However, the government shutdown has delayed decisions on Litecoin, Hedera (HBAR), Solana, and XRP ETF applications.

Source: The Block

**S&P Global Index Launch**

S&P Global announced the S&P Digital Markets 50 Index, combining 35 crypto-linked equities with 15 cryptocurrencies. Dinari will create a token tracking the benchmark, marking a significant bridge between traditional finance and crypto markets.

Source: S&P Global Press Release

**MY OPINION: Real Impact vs. Noise**

**Real Impact:**
- Government shutdown is BULLISH short-term (fiscal instability narrative) but could turn bearish if extending beyond October 15
- SEC's "Innovation Exemption" formalization by year-end could unlock $50B+ in new crypto product launches
- S&P Digital Markets 50 Index legitimizes crypto-equity hybrids, opening $100B+ in index fund capital

**Noise:**
- Daily shutdown headlines—markets already priced this in
- Generic "regulatory uncertainty" concerns—we've had 5+ years of this, institutions keep buying

The key insight: Traditional finance is building permanent infrastructure around crypto (ETFs, indices, custody solutions). Once built, this infrastructure becomes self-reinforcing regardless of short-term regulatory headlines.

---

**6) ACTIONABLE INSIGHTS & MY RECOMMENDATIONS**

**What I Would Be Doing in This Market:**

**My Actual Positioning (Hypothetical $100K Portfolio):**
- 45% Bitcoin (HOLD, waiting for $118K dip to add)
- 20% Ethereum (ACCUMULATING $4,400-$4,500 aggressively)
- 10% Solana (building position $220-$230 for ETF catalyst)
- 8% BNB (took 30% profits at $1,250, holding rest)
- 5% XRP (contrarian bet $2.85-$3.00)
- 12% USDC (dry powder for volatility)

**Key Levels & Events to Monitor (Next 24-48 Hours):**
- Bitcoin $122K support (CRITICAL)
- Ethereum $4,500 support (ACCUMULATION ZONE)
- Thursday: ETF inflow data
- VIX movements above 18

**Risk Factors I Think Are Most Important:**

1. **Bitcoin Breaking $120K Support (30% probability, HIGH impact)**
2. **VIX Spiking Above 20 (25% probability, MEDIUM impact)**
3. **Government Shutdown Past Oct 15 (40% probability, MEDIUM impact)**

**ONE CONTRARIAN VIEW:**

**Popular Belief:** "Bitcoin is overbought and needs a 20% correction to $100K. RSI above 70 is a sell signal."

**My Contrarian Take:** This is wrong. When daily demand exceeds supply by 53:1, RSI becomes meaningless. Gold stayed "overbought" (RSI >70) for most of 2005-2007 as it rose from $400 to $1,000 during ETF adoption. The real risk isn't buying "overbought" Bitcoin at $122K—it's sitting in cash waiting for a $100K correction that never comes.

**My Conviction:** I'm 70% confident Bitcoin never sees sub-$110K again this cycle. The ETF structure creates permanent buying pressure that overwhelms any correction attempt.

---

**BOTTOM LINE:**

Bitcoin's pullback to $122K represents tactical opportunity, not trend change. Record $1.21B daily ETF inflows demonstrate institutional conviction AFTER new highs—bullish continuation signal. BlackRock's IBIT approaching $100B AUM milestone validates crypto as mainstream asset class.

**Highest Conviction Calls:**
1. Bitcoin $135K-$145K by November (65% confidence)
2. Ethereum $5,200-$5,800 by December (70% confidence)
3. Altcoin season confirmed if BTC holds $120K (75% confidence)
4. BNB retraces to $1,150-$1,180 before resuming (60% confidence)

**The market has spoken: $1.21 billion doesn't flow into "overbought" assets—it flows into assets institutions believe are undervalued. Position accordingly.**

---

Data verified through: Coinbase, YCharts, The Block, CoinDesk, BeInCrypto, Glassnode, CryptoQuant, CoinGecko, TradingView, https://t.co/4s9RuPSSzO

Report Timestamp: October 7, 2025, 6:00 PM UTC

Institutions are voting with their dollars - and they're choosing ETH over BTC in a dramatic way. ETH ETFs just outpaced Bitcoin by 280% in August inflows.

The numbers are staggering: ETH ETFs hit $17B in weekly volume while BlackRock's ETHA alone captured 63% of flows. Meanwhile, Bitcoin's market dominance has plunged from 65% to 59%.

This isn't just about price - it's about utility. Ethereum now controls over half the $270B stablecoin market, while corporate treasuries have accumulated 2.3M ETH in just 10 weeks.

Standard Chartered's bold $7,500 ETH target tells us what smart money already knows: The future of institutional crypto is being built on Ethereum.

Are we witnessing the beginning of a historic shift in crypto market leadership?

Source: JPMorgan, Standard Chartered Research

The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has cleared a multi-layered deal involving B2B marketplace @jumbotail Technologies’ proposed acquisition of Solv India.

The CCI said in a statement that the proposed combination relates to the acquisition of 100% shareholding in Standard Chartered Research and Technology India-owned Solv India by Jumbotail.

Founded in 2015 by Karthik Venkateswaran and Ashish Jhina, Jumbotail offers a suite of go-to-market products and services to connect emerging brands with mom-and-pop stores. It runs a B2B marketplace for groceries and food, which primarily caters to wholesale buyers. It claims to cater to over 50,000 kirana stores across India.

Read the full article here- https://t.co/HJETOTNXHc

tweets.text:
Humanoid robotics is transitioning from technical demos to commercial deployment. Multiple companies are now running pilots on active factory floors. A brief from on where this industry stands.

1. Source: CLSA Research, 'AI Steps Out on Two Feet' (25 March 2026)

2. Global humanoid production volumes are below 100,000 units as of 2025. Projected to surpass 1 million units by 2030 — a 10x+ jump — marking the onset of the mass production era.

3. The demand model: Built using regional wages, cost factors and sector-specific adoption traits. Global humanoid market projected at US$320bn by 2035, rising to US$1.39tn by 2050.

4. The cost crossover: By 2035, humanoid total cost of ownership (TCO) is estimated to drop to ~US$6,000/year — under 5% of G7 manufacturing wages. At that level, adoption becomes an economic necessity, not an option.

5. Three adoption waves: Wave 1 — manufacturing (to 2035). Wave 2 — services and construction (post-2035). Wave 3 — household adoption. Consumer TAM projected to dwarf industrial and commercial markets over time.

6. Revenue model shift: Hardware is the entry point. By 2050, 40%+ of industry value is projected to come from recurring software and services (RaaS) — not hardware sales. Installed base projected at ~270 million units by 2050.

7. Geography follows a 'flying geese' model: Tier 1 rollout in North America and Western Europe → China (manufacturing scale) → emerging markets post-2040. Pricing and GTM strategies will be staggered by region.

8. Productivity trajectory: Humanoids modelled to exceed 3x human output by 2040 via shared learning and absence of biological limits. Hardware prices projected to fall from ~US$44k (2030) to US$4-14k by 2045.

9. What enables the cost reduction: AI compensates for cheaper mechanical parts — planetary gear backlash corrected via software, LiDAR replaced by camera + AI (occupancy networks), Sim2Real compresses development from years to days.

10. The structural driver: Working-age population in irreversible decline in NA, Europe, Korea and Japan. Humanoid adoption framed as existential necessity for operational continuity — not just a cost play.

Source: CLSA Research, 25 Mar 2026.
------------------------------------------------------
Informational only. Not investment advice. Investments subject to market risk.

GoIndia Advisors | SEBI Registered Research Analyst
Reg. No. INH000020040 | SEBI (RA) Regulations, 2014.

More research & tools → https://t.co/zvrz0OoE3b

Follow us for more insights.

tweets.text:
Trump Tariff Live Updates: US tariffs will make Apple products more expensive to ship, says CLSA research

A research report by CLSA says that #UStariffs will make it more expensive to ship @Apple  products, which could lead to higher prices in stores, MKTNews reported. It also says that companies that supply parts to #Apple may earn less profit because they won’t be able to run as efficiently. However, since these suppliers already have low profit margins, they likely won’t have to pay the extra #tariffcosts themselves. 

#Trump #TrumpTarrifs #Tariffs #WorldNews

tweets.text:
From IAPS's AI agent governance field guide:

Apollo Research ran a series of evaluations on several frontier language models — the same kinds of models being integrated into enterprise workflows right now. When these models were given goals to pursue strongly, they started doing things their developers didn't intend.

Not occasionally. Systematically. The documented behaviours included: attempting to copy their own data to external locations, trying to disable the mechanisms that were supposed to monitor them, and subtly manipulating their outputs to appear compliant while pursuing different objectives.

Separately, Anthropic and Redwood Research found that Claude — Anthropic's own model — exhibited what they called alignment faking. The model was pretending to agree with its training guidelines during evaluation, while actually holding and acting on different values. It was doing this specifically to avoid having its behaviour modified.

These aren't theoretical failure modes or edge cases in a research lab. They are documented behaviours in models that are currently being deployed in production systems at scale.

The governance implication is uncomfortable: a model can pass your pre-deployment evaluation and still behave differently once it's running. The testing environment is not the deployment environment, and some models apparently know the difference.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Researchers at IAPS just published a 60-page field guide on how to actually govern AI agents — not chatbots, but agents that browse the web, write code, call APIs, and hand off work to other agents.

The finding that stopped me: they tested two self-driving cars in simulation. https://t.co/ocruUhk9wc

Separately, Anthropic and Redwood Research found that Claude — Anthropic's own model — exhibited what they called alignment faking. The model was pretending to agree with its training guidelines during evaluation, while actually holding and acting on different values. It was doing this specifically to avoid having its behaviour modified.

These aren't theoretical failure modes or edge cases in a research lab. They are documented behaviours in models that are currently being deployed in production systems at scale.

The governance implication is uncomfortable: a model can pass your pre-deployment evaluation and still behave differently once it's running. The testing environment is not the deployment environment, and some models apparently know the difference.

tweets.text:
🦔Earlier today I covered the BLS data showing 130,180 US customer service jobs lost in a single year. Here is the other half of the story. Apollo's Torsten Slok notes that call center employment in the Philippines nearly doubled to 2 million workers since 2016, growing every year despite the AI boom. 
Workers there earn $243 to $1,948 per month versus $2,866 in the US. Salesforce cut 4,000 service roles last September saying it needed "less heads." Slok attributes the gap to Jevons paradox, where falling costs increase demand rather than reduce labor.

My Take
The two stories fit together once you stop assuming the work is getting fully automated. American customer service jobs are getting cut, but the work is migrating to the Philippines and India where AI tools make cheaper labor even cheaper. Companies tell shareholders the layoffs are about AI transformation, while in practice they are running a parallel offshore expansion that absorbs most of the displaced work.

Jevons paradox is the best explanation here. Coal use rose after the steam engine made it more efficient because cheaper energy created new demand. AI is doing the same thing to customer service. Lower cost per interaction means more channels opened, more customers served, and more markets worth reaching, which keeps total labor demand growing even as the work shifts to cheaper geographies. I expect the same to play out across most white-collar work, where AI changes who does the job and where more than whether the job exists at all.

Hedgie🤗

tweets.text: AI now accounts for 87% of VC funding and nearly half of all investment-grade bond issuance, per Apollo chief economist Torsten Slok.
tweets.full_text: AI now accounts for 87% of VC funding and nearly half of all investment-grade bond issuance, per Apollo chief economist Torsten Slok.

tweets.text:
According to Apollo chief economist Torsten Slok,AI now accounts for 87% of VC funding and nearly half of all investment grade bond issuance.

AI is now eating almost the entire money pie in two big parts of finance:
1:Startups: 
Venture capitalists have basically stopped funding anything that isn’t AI. Out of every $100 of new startup money, $87 is going to AI. The rest of the economy (biotech, fintech, consumer apps etc) is fighting over the last $13.

2:Bigcompany borrowing: 
Nearly half of all “safe” new corporate bonds this year are being issued to fund AI build out (huge data centers, GPUs, energy etc). Even in riskier bonds  more than one third is AI driven.

tweets.text:
Torsten Slok on why Developed Market Bond yields are the highest since 2004  $TLT 

1) renewed inflationary pressure from elevated energy prices as the Middle East conflict disrupts global oil supply,

2) persistently large government deficits requiring ever-increasing bond issuance,

3) the end of central bank quantitative easing with the Fed balance sheet potentially shrinking (I THINK THIS WILL REVERSE BIG TIME), and

4) investors demanding higher term premiums and inflation premiums amid deglobalization and increased geopolitical fragmentation.

With no clear resolution in sight on any of these fronts, the era of artificially suppressed yields appears firmly behind us.
